all_rules.rule: Pin fastapi@0.115.x, pydantic@2.9.x or 2.10.x, uvicorn[standard]@0.32.x, starlette>=0.40,<1.0, and Python >=3.12,<3.13. Do not mix pydantic v1 into this stack.
all_rules.rationale: FastAPI 0.115 requires pydantic v2 only; the version compatibility table pins these ranges together to avoid breakage.

all_rules.rule: Write endpoints as `async def` whenever they touch the database, HTTP, or WebSockets so they can use async drivers without blocking the event loop.
all_rules.rationale: Async endpoints integrate with async DB drivers (aiosqlite/asyncpg) as shown in Example 3; sync handlers forfeit concurrency benefits.

all_rules.rule: Never call blocking/CPU-bound code (pandas, numpy, heavy loops) directly inside `async def`. Wrap with `await asyncio.to_thread(...)` or use a sync `def` endpoint (FastAPI runs those in a threadpool).
all_rules.rationale: Gotcha 3.1 explicitly warns that blocking calls inside async freeze the event loop.

all_rules.rule: Use pydantic v2 APIs only: `.model_dump()` (not `.dict()`), `.model_validate()` (not `.parse_obj()`), and `model_config = ConfigDict(...)` (not inner `Config` class).
all_rules.rationale: Gotcha 3.2 flags these as the pydantic v2 migration points; v1 calls will raise or silently mis-serialize.

all_rules.rule: For any response model populated from ORM objects, set `model_config = ConfigDict(from_attributes=True)`. Do not use v1's `orm_mode = True`.
all_rules.rationale: Shown in MarketOut (Example 2); gotcha 3.3 confirms `from_attributes=True` is the v2 replacement.

all_rules.rule: For PATCH-style partial updates, call `.model_dump(exclude_unset=True)` so unset fields are not written with defaults.
all_rules.rationale: Gotcha 3.4 notes default behavior changed in v2; PATCH semantics require explicit exclude_unset.

all_rules.rule: Always declare `response_model=` on routes that return ORM objects (e.g. `response_model=list[MarketOut]`). Missing it leaks internal DB fields to the client.
all_rules.rationale: Gotcha 3.7 explicitly warns forgotten response_model leaks DB fields; Examples 3 show the pattern.

all_rules.rule: Constrain numeric query/body fields with pydantic `Field(ge=..., le=..., gt=...)` instead of runtime checks. E.g. probabilities use `Field(ge=0, le=1)`, bankroll uses `Field(gt=0)`.
all_rules.rationale: Example 2 uses Field constraints on yes_price/no_price/volume_24h/bankroll for automatic validation and OpenAPI docs.

all_rules.rule: Use `typing.Literal` for fixed enumerations like Platform = Literal["kalshi","polymarket","manifold","predictit"] rather than free-form strings.
all_rules.rationale: Example 2 defines Platform as a Literal so FastAPI validates and documents the allowed values.

all_rules.rule: Expose the DB session as a dependency via `async def get_session() -> AsyncIterator[AsyncSession]` using `async with SessionLocal() as session: yield session`, then inject with `session: AsyncSession = Depends(get_session)`.
all_rules.rationale: Example 4 shows the canonical DI pattern; gotcha 3.5 warns not to wrap it in @asynccontextmanager.

all_rules.rule: Do NOT decorate DI yield-based dependencies with `@asynccontextmanager`. FastAPI's `Depends` expects a plain async generator returning an `AsyncIterator`.
all_rules.rationale: Gotcha 3.5 explicitly prohibits wrapping yield-based Depends in asynccontextmanager.

all_rules.rule: Manage engine lifecycle with FastAPI lifespan: define `@asynccontextmanager async def lifespan(app)` with startup before `yield` and `await engine.dispose()` after. Pass via `FastAPI(..., lifespan=lifespan)`.
all_rules.rationale: Example 1 shows lifespan replacing deprecated on_event startup/shutdown hooks and properly disposing the engine.

all_rules.rule: Add `CORSMiddleware` before mounting routers and list explicit origins (e.g. `http://localhost:3000`). Adding it after routers means error responses miss CORS headers.
all_rules.rationale: Gotcha 3.6 explicitly calls out middleware ordering; Example 1 shows CORS added before include_router.

all_rules.rule: Return `HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="...")` on missing resources rather than returning None or 200 with empty body.
all_rules.rationale: Example 3 `get_market` raises 404 via HTTPException when session.get returns None.

all_rules.rule: For job-start endpoints (backtests etc.) return HTTP 202 Accepted with a `{job_id, status}` body; do not block the request on the work.
all_rules.rationale: Example 5 uses `@router.post("", status_code=202)` and returns job_id/status for the async backtest kickoff.

all_rules.rule: Use `BackgroundTasks` only for fire-and-forget in-process work tied to a single response. For durable, retryable, or cross-worker queues use Celery / RQ / APScheduler.
all_rules.rationale: Gotcha 3.8 warns BackgroundTasks run in the same worker after response and are not durable.

all_rules.rule: Prefer WebSockets (`@router.websocket`) for high-frequency live data such as per-market tick streams; use regular REST + polling for low-frequency summaries.
all_rules.rationale: Example 6 defines `/stream/{market_id}` pushing ticks with `await asyncio.sleep(0.25)`, the intended live-tick pattern.

all_rules.rule: Always handle `WebSocketDisconnect` inside WebSocket endpoints (try/except around the send loop) so disconnects don't raise to the ASGI layer.
all_rules.rationale: Example 6 wraps the stream loop in try/except WebSocketDisconnect.

all_rules.rule: Register custom domain exceptions (e.g. `AdapterError`) via `@app.exception_handler(...)` returning a `JSONResponse` with a 502 upstream-failure status for adapter errors.
all_rules.rationale: Example 7 shows AdapterError mapped to status 502 with `{platform, detail}` payload.

all_rules.rule: Test async endpoints with `httpx.AsyncClient(transport=ASGITransport(app=app))`; avoid starlette's sync `TestClient` which has edge cases with lifespan.
all_rules.rationale: Section 4 explicitly recommends httpx AsyncClient + ASGITransport over TestClient.

all_rules.rule: Do not ship production with `uvicorn --reload`; reload is dev-only.
all_rules.rationale: Gotcha 3.9 warns against reload in production.

all_rules.rule: Use SQLAlchemy 2.x typed declarative style: subclass `DeclarativeBase` and declare columns as `Mapped[T] = mapped_column(...)`. Do not use the pre-2.0 `Column(...)` / legacy declarative_base pattern.
all_rules.rationale: Section 1 mandates typed Mapped[] style; Example 2 models all use `Mapped[...] = mapped_column(...)`.

all_rules.rule: Create the async engine with `create_async_engine(url, pool_pre_ping=True)` and a session factory via `async_sessionmaker(engine, expire_on_commit=False, class_=AsyncSession)`.
all_rules.rationale: Example 1 shows this exact configuration; gotcha 3.2 explains expire_on_commit=False is recommended for web contexts.

all_rules.rule: Use `sqlite+aiosqlite:///...` for local dev. The upgrade path to Postgres is `postgresql+asyncpg://user:pass@host/db` with increased `pool_size=10, max_overflow=20`.
all_rules.rationale: Example 1 documents DATABASE_URL and gotcha 3.8 describes the Postgres upgrade path.

all_rules.rule: Never rely on implicit lazy loading in async code. Eager-load relationships with `.options(selectinload(Model.relation))` or `joinedload(...)` in the initial query.
all_rules.rationale: Section 1 and gotcha 3.1 state lazy access after session close raises `MissingGreenlet`; Example 4 shows selectinload usage.

all_rules.rule: Do not share an `AsyncSession` across asyncio tasks. Scope one session per request / per task via the Depends(get_session) pattern.
all_rules.rationale: Gotcha 3.5 forbids sharing AsyncSession across tasks.

all_rules.rule: For primary-key lookups use `await session.get(Model, pk)`; for unique single-row queries use `.scalar_one_or_none()` rather than `.first()`.
all_rules.rationale: Gotchas 3.6 and 3.7 prefer scalar_one_or_none and session.get for clarity and speed.

all_rules.rule: Use the 2.0 unified statement API: `select()/insert()/update()/delete()` passed to `await session.execute(stmt)`; extract rows with `.scalars().all()` or `.scalar_one_or_none()`.
all_rules.rationale: Section 1 and Example 3 use the unified API exclusively (e.g. `select(Market).limit(limit)` then `result.scalars().all()`).

all_rules.rule: For bulk insert of high-volume rows (e.g. price ticks), use `insert(Model).values([...])` with a single `await session.execute(stmt)` + one commit, not a loop of `session.add`.
all_rules.rationale: Example 5 demonstrates the bulk-insert performance path for tick ingestion.

all_rules.rule: For multi-statement transactions use `async with session.begin():` — it auto-commits on success and rolls back on exception. Do not add a manual commit inside the block.
all_rules.rationale: Example 6 shows transaction scope via session.begin() with comment 'No explicit commit needed'.

all_rules.rule: Assume SQLite + aiosqlite serializes writes; do not design for concurrent write throughput on SQLite. Plan multi-writer workloads for the Postgres upgrade path.
all_rules.rationale: Gotcha 3.3 notes aiosqlite serialises writes with no real write concurrency.

all_rules.rule: Declare cascade on owning relationships where deletes should propagate (e.g. `relationship(back_populates=..., cascade="all, delete-orphan")`) and pair with `ForeignKey(..., ondelete="CASCADE")` on the child.
all_rules.rationale: Example 2 Market.ticks uses cascade='all, delete-orphan' with PriceTick.market_id FK ondelete='CASCADE'.

all_rules.rule: Add composite indexes via `__table_args__ = (Index("ix_...", col_a, col_b),)` for frequent range queries (e.g. market_id + ts for tick lookups).
all_rules.rationale: Example 2 PriceTick defines Index('ix_market_ts', 'market_id', 'ts') for the tick-by-market-time access pattern.

all_rules.rule: Pin `alembic@1.13.x` paired with SQLAlchemy 2.x; older alembic versions do not support the Mapped[] typed metadata correctly.
all_rules.rationale: Section 1 and the compatibility table pin alembic ^1.13 as SQLAlchemy 2.x compatible.

all_rules.rule: For async SQLAlchemy, `env.py` MUST use `async_engine_from_config(..., poolclass=pool.NullPool)` with `connection.run_sync(do_run_migrations)` inside `asyncio.run(run_migrations_online())`. The plain sync template will not work with aiosqlite/asyncpg.
all_rules.rationale: Section 2 env.py template and gotcha 4.5 explicitly require the async pattern.

all_rules.rule: Configure `context.configure(..., render_as_batch=True)` in `do_run_migrations` whenever the target DB is SQLite; without it column drops/alters fail.
all_rules.rationale: Gotcha 4.1 and the env.py comment state render_as_batch is essential for SQLite table-rewrite operations.

all_rules.rule: Wrap any SQLite ALTER/DROP column operation in `with op.batch_alter_table("t") as batch:` — SQLite cannot ALTER COLUMN natively.
all_rules.rationale: Example 2 and gotcha 4.1 show batch_alter_table as the required pattern.

all_rules.rule: Keep data migrations separate in logic: add column nullable, run `op.get_bind().execute(sa.text(...))` to backfill, then use batch_alter_table to set NOT NULL and add constraints.
all_rules.rationale: Example 3 demonstrates the add-backfill-tighten pattern for adding a non-null `slug`.

all_rules.rule: Set `target_metadata = Base.metadata` in env.py AND import the module(s) declaring every model before autogenerate runs, or autogenerate will silently miss tables.
all_rules.rationale: Gotcha 4.4 warns the models module must be imported for autogenerate to see all tables.

all_rules.rule: Always review autogenerated migrations before committing — autogenerate misses CheckConstraint, server-default mismatches, and enum changes.
all_rules.rationale: Gotcha 4.2 enumerates what autogenerate misses and mandates manual review.

all_rules.rule: Never edit a migration that has been applied to a shared database; create a new forward migration instead.
all_rules.rationale: Gotcha 4.3 explicitly forbids mutating applied migrations.

all_rules.rule: Run `alembic upgrade head` in CI before the test suite so tests execute against current schema.
all_rules.rationale: Gotcha 4.7 mandates CI runs upgrade head before tests.

all_rules.rule: When migrating SQLite to Postgres, dump data, run fresh migrations on Postgres, then restore via SQL. Do not rely on Alembic to migrate data across dialects.
all_rules.rationale: Gotcha 4.6 states Alembic does not migrate data across dialects.

all_rules.rule: Use `alembic upgrade head --sql > upgrade.sql` to preview DDL before applying to production databases.
all_rules.rationale: Example 4 CLI recipe shows offline SQL generation for pre-deployment review.

all_rules.rule: Use the App Router (app/ directory) exclusively; do not create a pages/ directory. Route segments must be organized as app/<route>/page.tsx with layout.tsx, loading.tsx, error.tsx, and not-found.tsx conventions.
all_rules.rationale: Next.js 15 defaults to App Router and the project tech-kb standardizes on it. Mixing Pages Router creates two routing models and duplicate data-fetching paradigms.

all_rules.rule: Components are Server Components by default. Only add the "use client" directive when the component needs browser APIs, event handlers, React state/effects, or next/navigation client hooks. Push the client boundary as deep in the tree as possible.
all_rules.rationale: tech-kb: '"use client" is viral downward for bundling but components imported above can still be Server Components — keep the boundary as deep as possible.' Preserves RSC bundle savings.

all_rules.rule: In route segments, type params and searchParams as Promise<...> and await them. Do not treat them as synchronous objects. Same rule for cookies(), headers(), and draftMode().
all_rules.rationale: Breaking change in Next 15: these request APIs are now async. tech-kb Example 4 shows the required Promise-typed PageProps pattern.

all_rules.rule: Every fetch() call must explicitly declare its caching behavior: either next: { revalidate: n } for ISR, cache: 'force-cache' for static, or cache: 'no-store' for dynamic. Do not rely on implicit caching.
all_rules.rationale: Next 15 removed default fetch caching. tech-kb gotcha: '`fetch` is uncached by default in 15. Explicitly opt in.' Explicit intent avoids accidental staleness or per-request over-fetching.

all_rules.rule: Route handlers (app/**/route.ts) that read headers, cookies, or request-specific search params must declare `export const dynamic = 'force-dynamic'`. Handlers that proxy user-scoped backend calls must never be statically cached.
all_rules.rationale: tech-kb: 'Route handlers need dynamic = "force-dynamic" when you read headers or use query params that change per request; otherwise they get cached statically at build.'

all_rules.rule: Server Actions must live in files marked with "use server" at the top, must validate all FormData input before use, and must call revalidatePath / revalidateTag for any affected route after mutation.
all_rules.rationale: tech-kb Example 5 demonstrates the pattern. Without revalidation, downstream Server Components show stale data; without validation, untrusted FormData reaches the backend.

all_rules.rule: Set reactStrictMode: true and experimental.typedRoutes: true in next.config.ts. Backend proxy paths must be declared via rewrites() (e.g. /api/backend/:path* -> 127.0.0.1:8000/api/:path*), not hardcoded in client code.
all_rules.rationale: tech-kb Example 6 shows this exact config. Typed routes catches dead links at compile time; centralized rewrite keeps the backend origin configurable.

all_rules.rule: Data-fetching page components should wrap their rendering children in <Suspense fallback={...}> so streaming works. Prefer passing fetched data into a presentational child over fetching inside a client child.
all_rules.rationale: tech-kb Example 1 (MarketsPage) wraps MarketTable in Suspense; this enables React 19 streaming SSR and preserves the server/client split.

all_rules.rule: tsconfig.json must have strict: true, noUncheckedIndexedAccess: true, noImplicitOverride: true, exactOptionalPropertyTypes: true, noFallthroughCasesInSwitch: true, and isolatedModules: true. allowJs must be false.
all_rules.rationale: tech-kb section 2 recommends these exact flags for frontend tsconfig. noUncheckedIndexedAccess and exactOptionalPropertyTypes catch real bugs on market/tick data arrays and optional fields.

all_rules.rule: Do not use `any`. Use `unknown` for untrusted inputs and narrow via type guards, zod parsing, or discriminated-union switches. Exhaustive switches must end with `const _: never = x` default for compile-time exhaustiveness.
all_rules.rationale: tech-kb gotcha: 'Avoid any; prefer unknown then narrow.' Example 1 shows the `never` exhaustiveness pattern for Order state unions.

all_rules.rule: All data crossing the network boundary (responses from FastAPI, Server Action inputs, route handler bodies) must be validated with a zod schema via Schema.parse() or Schema.safeParse(). Derive TypeScript types with z.infer<typeof Schema>.
all_rules.rationale: tech-kb Example 2 (MarketSchema) pairs zod runtime validation with z.infer so the type and runtime shape cannot drift. Without this, backend schema changes silently corrupt the UI.

all_rules.rule: Use `as const satisfies T` for constant object literals (registries, config maps). Do not use `as T` casts — they widen and hide errors. Reserved for cases where `satisfies` genuinely cannot express the intent.
all_rules.rationale: tech-kb gotcha: 'as const satisfies T is better than as T — satisfies does not widen literals.' Example 3 shows the STRATEGIES registry pattern used to derive StrategyId = keyof typeof STRATEGIES.

all_rules.rule: Pin zod to ^3.23.x. Do not mix zod v3 and v4 in the same project. @types/react must be pinned to ^19 to match React 19 event types.
all_rules.rationale: tech-kb gotchas: 'Don't mix zod versions — v4 has breaking API changes' and 'In React 19, event types from @types/react@19 differ subtly from 18 — pin @types/react@^19.'

all_rules.rule: Prefer Result<T,E> or ok/err discriminated unions over throwing for expected failures at I/O boundaries (fetch, parse). Reserve thrown exceptions for genuinely unexpected conditions.
all_rules.rationale: tech-kb Example 4 defines Result<T,E> with safeFetch — makes error paths explicit in the type system and forces call sites to handle them.

all_rules.rule: Use Tailwind CSS v4 CSS-first config. Do not create tailwind.config.js or tailwind.config.ts. Theme tokens live in an @theme { ... } block inside app/globals.css. CSS entry must be `@import "tailwindcss";` — never the old @tailwind base/components/utilities triad.
all_rules.rationale: tech-kb section 1 mandates CSS-first config; gotcha: 'No more content: [] array — delete the old config file when upgrading from v3.'

all_rules.rule: Because the deployment theme is dark-only, force dark mode with `@variant dark (&:where(:root, :root *));` in globals.css and set color-scheme: dark on :root. Do not ship a light-mode toggle or rely on prefers-color-scheme.
all_rules.rationale: tech-kb section 2 example shows the exact @variant directive. Comment: 'Force dark mode always — deployment.theme = "dark-only"'.

all_rules.rule: All colors, spacing, radii, and fonts must be sourced from the @theme tokens (--color-yes, --color-no, --color-accent, --color-bg, --color-surface, --color-text, --spacing-gutter, --radius-card, etc.) defined in globals.css. Do not hardcode hex colors in className strings; prefer theme-named utilities (text-yes, bg-surface) or var(--color-*).
all_rules.rationale: tech-kb gotcha: 'Arbitrary values bg-[#123] still work; prefer theme tokens for consistency.' Shared palette is required for chart components to visually match UI.

all_rules.rule: For shared styles, use the @utility directive (e.g. @utility live-dot) rather than @apply in component CSS. Use oklch()/lab() color functions over hex for new token additions.
all_rules.rationale: tech-kb: '@apply still works but is discouraged in v4 for shared styles — use @utility instead' and 'Colors in v4 accept oklch()/lab() — prefer them over hex for perceptually uniform palettes.'

all_rules.rule: Numeric content (prices in cents, volumes, PnL, timestamps) must render with font-mono or tabular-nums so digits align and do not jitter on updates. Pair with right-aligned text for columnar data.
all_rules.rationale: tech-kb Example 3 table row uses font-mono text-right for pnl; Example 1 uses font-mono for YES/NO prices. Prediction-market UIs update in place and non-tabular digits shift visibly.

all_rules.rule: Do not install tailwindcss@3 alongside v4 plugins. PostCSS plugin is @tailwindcss/postcss (separate package in v4). Do not add autoprefixer — Lightning CSS handles it.
all_rules.rationale: tech-kb gotcha + version table: 'autoprefixer NOT NEEDED in v4 — Lightning CSS handles it' and 'Don't install tailwindcss@3 and v4 plugins side-by-side.'

all_rules.rule: Every file that renders a Recharts chart must begin with "use client". Chart components must never be imported directly into Server Components; wrap them in a thin client component.
all_rules.rationale: tech-kb gotcha: 'Must be Client Component — add "use client". Recharts uses browser SVG APIs.' Section 4: 'Route chart components through "use client" wrappers; keep page shells Server Components.'

all_rules.rule: Wrap charts in <ResponsiveContainer width="100%" height={N}> with an explicit height (or place inside a parent with a defined height). Never use ResponsiveContainer inside an unsized flex/grid cell.
all_rules.rationale: tech-kb gotcha: 'ResponsiveContainer needs a parent with a defined height (or explicit height prop). A common bug is "chart doesn't render" — check parent has height.'

all_rules.rule: For time-series axes use type="number", scale="time", numeric Unix-ms timestamps in dataKey, and a tickFormatter that calls new Date(v).toLocale...(). Do not use string timestamps as x values.
all_rules.rationale: tech-kb gotcha: 'For time axes, use type="number" + scale="time" + numeric Unix-ms ts. Strings work but sort alphabetically.' Example 1 is the reference implementation.

all_rules.rule: Set isAnimationActive={false} on Line/Area/Bar for any chart that updates on live data (polled every 30s per live_poll_interval_seconds). Set dot={false} on Line charts with more than ~100 points.
all_rules.rationale: tech-kb gotchas: 'isAnimationActive={false} for real-time data to avoid animation stutter on each update' and 'dot={false} for lines with >100 points — per-point dots tank rendering.'

all_rules.rule: Chart colors must be sourced from a shared chartTheme.ts object whose values mirror the Tailwind @theme tokens (--color-yes, --color-no, --color-accent, --color-border, --color-text-muted). Do not hardcode hex strings in chart props.
all_rules.rationale: tech-kb section 4: 'Share a chartTheme.ts object with colors matching Tailwind tokens to avoid hard-coding hex strings.' Keeps palette in one source of truth.

all_rules.rule: Tooltip styling must be set via the contentStyle inline prop, not via className. Tooltip labelFormatter / formatter callbacks must return the same unit the YAxis displays (e.g. cents with ¢ suffix for prices).
all_rules.rationale: tech-kb gotcha: 'Tooltip contentStyle must be inline; class-based styling is unreliable.' Example 1 shows the formatter returning `${(v * 100).toFixed(2)}¢` to match the YAxis tickFormatter.

all_rules.rule: Pin recharts to ^2.13.x. Do not upgrade to recharts@3.x. For live-updating data feeds use SWR or react-query with refetchInterval: 30_000 (matches live_poll_interval_seconds) — do not re-implement polling with raw setInterval inside chart components.
all_rules.rationale: tech-kb gotcha: 'recharts@3.x is available but has breaking changes — stick to 2.13.x for stability.' Section 4 specifies the 30s poll interval aligned with data_refresh_policy.

all_rules.rule: When multiple charts share an x-axis (e.g. price + volume for the same market), give them the same syncId so hover crosshairs align. Use <ReferenceLine> / <ReferenceArea> for event annotations (news spikes, resolution times) rather than a separate chart.
all_rules.rationale: tech-kb Example 4 demonstrates syncId for linked price/volume charts; Example 6 shows ReferenceArea+ReferenceLine for news annotations.

all_rules.rule: Price series MUST be represented as pandas.Series indexed by DatetimeIndex constructed with utc=True.
all_rules.rationale: Mixing naive and tz-aware datetimes breaks joins/resample; UTC is the project canonical timezone.

all_rules.rule: Prediction market prices MUST be stored as decimal probabilities in [0, 1] (yes/no columns), not percentages.
all_rules.rationale: Brier score and Kelly formulas assume p in [0,1]; downstream resampling (ohlc) and forecasts expect this scale.

all_rules.rule: Simple returns MUST use pandas.Series.pct_change(); log returns MUST use np.log(prices / prices.shift(1)).dropna(). Do not substitute one for the other.
all_rules.rationale: pct_change yields simple returns; log returns are required for additive compounding across periods.

all_rules.rule: Sharpe ratio MUST annualise using sqrt(periods_per_year) * mean(excess) / std(excess, ddof=1), where excess = returns - rf/periods_per_year.
all_rules.rationale: Canonical annualised Sharpe formula per Example 2; ddof=1 matches sample std (Excel STDEV) finance convention.

all_rules.rule: Sharpe implementations MUST guard against sigma == 0 or np.isnan(sigma) by returning 0.0 instead of dividing.
all_rules.rationale: Zero-volatility and all-NaN windows otherwise produce inf/NaN, poisoning downstream aggregation.

all_rules.rule: Sortino ratio MUST define downside deviation as sqrt(mean(excess[excess < 0] ** 2)), NOT the std of negative returns.
all_rules.rationale: Example 3 defines dd as RMS of negative excess returns; using std(negatives) silently changes semantics.

all_rules.rule: Sortino implementations MUST return 0.0 when downside deviation is 0 or NaN.
all_rules.rationale: Periods with no losses otherwise produce inf; return 0.0 keeps ratios comparable and aggregable.

all_rules.rule: Max drawdown MUST be computed as (equity / equity.cummax()) - 1.0, with trough = dd.idxmin() and peak = equity.loc[:trough].idxmax(). Returns MUST be (float(dd.min()), peak, trough).
all_rules.rationale: Example 4 fixes the canonical algorithm; alternative peak-detection orderings silently produce wrong peak dates.

all_rules.rule: Brier score for binary outcomes MUST be mean((forecasts - outcomes) ** 2) where forecasts in [0,1] and outcomes in {0,1}.
all_rules.rationale: Standard Brier definition per Example 5; applies to YES-probability forecasts only.

all_rules.rule: Brier Skill Score MUST use the realised outcome mean as climatology when climatology is None, and MUST return 0.0 when the reference Brier is 0.
all_rules.rationale: Example 5 uses outcomes.mean() as default climatology; zero-reference guard prevents division by zero.

all_rules.rule: Kelly fraction for binary payoff MUST be f = (b * p_win - q) / b where q = 1 - p_win and b = net odds, and MUST be clamped to [0.0, 1.0].
all_rules.rationale: Example 6 formula; clamping enforces full-Kelly cap and prevents negative (short) or >100% bankroll exposure.

all_rules.rule: Kelly implementations MUST return 0.0 when b <= 0 and MUST wrap divisions in np.errstate(divide='ignore', invalid='ignore') for vectorised variants.
all_rules.rationale: Example 6 guards against non-positive odds and suppresses NumPy warnings during masked vectorisation.

all_rules.rule: Rolling z-score MUST divide by sd.replace(0, np.nan) (ddof=1), never by raw std, to avoid silent inf values.
all_rules.rationale: Example 7 explicitly replaces zero std with NaN so downstream masks can drop degenerate windows.

all_rules.rule: Hot-path metric computations MUST be vectorised with .to_numpy() + numpy ops; pure-Python loops and DataFrame.iterrows() are BANNED for any series exceeding 1000 rows.
all_rules.rationale: iterrows is orders of magnitude slower and allocates per-row Series objects; project pitfall section mandates vectorisation.

all_rules.rule: Group-aggregations MUST prefer groupby(...).agg({...}) over groupby(...).apply(lambda ...).
all_rules.rationale: Performance tips section: .agg dispatches to C paths while .apply boxes each group through Python.

all_rules.rule: Metric functions MUST be pure: inputs are pandas/numpy objects, outputs are floats/tuples, NO file, network, or database I/O inside the function body.
all_rules.rationale: All Example 2-6 functions take Series and return scalars; keeps metrics unit-testable and deterministic.

all_rules.rule: Inputs to scipy.stats functions MUST be .dropna()'d before the call; do NOT rely on scipy to skip NaN.
all_rules.rationale: Gotcha section: scipy.stats returns NaN if ANY input is NaN, silently invalidating t-tests and p-values.

all_rules.rule: For mixed/object dtype Series use pd.isna; reserve np.isnan for strictly numeric float arrays.
all_rules.rationale: Gotcha section: np.isnan raises on object dtype; pd.isna is the safe default for heterogeneous columns.

all_rules.rule: Masked writes MUST use df.loc[mask, col] = value; chained assignment df[col][mask] = value is BANNED.
all_rules.rationale: Gotcha section: chained assignment silently fails with SettingWithCopyWarning, producing non-persistent writes.

all_rules.rule: Default risk-free rate for Sharpe/Sortino is rf = 0.0; default periods_per_year = 252. Any override MUST be passed explicitly, and for 24/7 prediction markets callers SHOULD use a market-appropriate factor (e.g., 365 for daily, 8760 for hourly, 525600 for 1-min bars) rather than 252.
all_rules.rationale: Example 2-3 pin defaults to 252 (equities trading-day convention); prediction markets trade continuously so annualisation factor must reflect bar cadence.

all_rules.rule: .rolling(window) outputs MUST be handled either via .dropna() or min_periods=1; consumers MUST NOT assume the first (window-1) rows are valid.
all_rules.rationale: Gotcha section: rolling emits NaN during warm-up; ignoring this contaminates downstream means and z-scores.

all_rules.rule: Tick dataframes MUST be set_index('ts').sort_index() after pd.to_datetime(..., utc=True) before any resample or join.
all_rules.rationale: Example 1 mandates this pipeline; unsorted DatetimeIndex causes incorrect resample buckets and resample-order bugs.

all_rules.rule: Equity curves MUST be built as starting_bankroll + fills.sort_values('ts')['pnl'].cumsum(), indexed by fill timestamp and named 'equity'.
all_rules.rationale: Example 10 canonical construction; unsorted fills produce non-monotonic equity and invalid drawdown calculations.

all_rules.rule: News-spike detection MUST use prices.pct_change(window).abs() >= trigger_pct with project defaults trigger_pct=0.10 and window_min=15.
all_rules.rationale: Example 8 pins default thresholds; diverging values across modules breaks alert reproducibility.

all_rules.rule: Version pins: pandas ^2.2, numpy ^1.26 (pin <2.0 unless all transitive deps confirmed NumPy-2-compatible), scipy ^1.14, python 3.12. Do NOT use np.cast or np.float_ (removed in NumPy 2.x); use np.float64.
all_rules.rationale: Version compatibility matrix + gotcha: NumPy 2.x is ABI-breaking and removed deprecated aliases; silent imports fail at runtime.

all_rules.rule: Pin testing tooling to the declared versions (pytest 8.3.x, pytest-asyncio 0.24.x, pytest-cov 5.0.x, httpx 0.27.x, Python 3.12; vitest 2.1.x, @testing-library/react 16.x, @playwright/test 1.48.x, Node 20 LTS) and do not upgrade ad hoc.
all_rules.rationale: Tech KB declares explicit version pins so async, React 19, and Playwright browser binaries stay mutually compatible; drift causes subtle fixture or JSX breakage.

all_rules.rule: Set asyncio_mode = "auto" in pyproject.toml [tool.pytest.ini_options] and use @pytest_asyncio.fixture (not @pytest.fixture) for any async fixture.
all_rules.rationale: asyncio_mode=auto removes the need to mark every async test, but fixtures still require the asyncio-aware decorator; mixing them silently skips setup.

all_rules.rule: Always configure pytest with addopts including -ra --strict-markers --strict-config and --cov=backend --cov-report=term-missing, and register every custom marker in pyproject.toml.
all_rules.rationale: --strict-markers prevents typo markers from silently being ignored; --strict-config surfaces misconfiguration; coverage output on every run keeps gaps visible.

all_rules.rule: Use @pytest.mark.parametrize to cover boundary cases explicitly (e.g., Kelly fraction at p=0.5, p<0.5 clamped to 0, p>0.5 positive) and assign readable ids via pytest.param(..., id="...").
all_rules.rationale: Parametrize with named ids surfaces exactly which boundary failed; the KB's kelly_fraction and sharpe examples show boundary + alternating cases are mandatory.

all_rules.rule: Compare floats with pytest.approx(..., rel=..., abs=...), never with ==.
all_rules.rationale: Direct float equality is listed as a gotcha that "will burn you"; approx pins tolerance explicitly.

all_rules.rule: Keep DB and HTTP fixtures function-scoped by default; only promote to session scope (like the in-memory async engine) when state must persist across the whole run, and always teardown with yield.
all_rules.rationale: Function-scope is the pytest default and prevents cross-test state leakage; session scope is reserved for expensive objects like the SQLAlchemy async engine.

all_rules.rule: Integration tests against FastAPI must use httpx.AsyncClient with ASGITransport(app=app) and base_url="http://test" inside an async context manager, not a live network server.
all_rules.rationale: ASGITransport runs the app in-process for deterministic, fast integration tests without binding a port; the KB uses this pattern for /api/markets.

all_rules.rule: Mock outbound adapter HTTP calls (Kalshi, Polymarket, etc.) with respx_mock inside async tests rather than hitting real APIs.
all_rules.rationale: respx mocks httpx at the transport layer so adapter unit/integration tests stay deterministic and offline-safe; the KB shows the polymarket fetch_market example.

all_rules.rule: Provide factory fixtures (e.g., make_market) that accept **overrides over sensible defaults; do not hand-construct model objects in each test.
all_rules.rationale: Factories centralize defaults (id, platform, prices, closes_at) so tests stay focused on the variable under test and resist schema churn.

all_rules.rule: Freeze time with @freeze_time (freezegun / pytest-freezegun) for any code that reads the current time, and seed randomness to prevent flaky runs.
all_rules.rationale: The KB's news_spike_cutoff test demonstrates deterministic time; freezing time and seeding RNG are the documented flakiness-prevention mechanisms.

all_rules.rule: Use monkeypatch.setenv for env var mutation inside tests; never mutate os.environ directly.
all_rules.rationale: monkeypatch auto-reverts at test teardown; direct os.environ edits leak state across tests.

all_rules.rule: Tag slow or external-dependency tests with @pytest.mark.integration or @pytest.mark.slow so the default fast run is `pytest -m "not integration and not slow"`.
all_rules.rationale: Marker-driven selection keeps CI fast tiers clean; the KB registers unit/integration/slow markers for exactly this purpose.

all_rules.rule: Enforce a minimum coverage gate of --cov-fail-under=80 in CI, and ensure critical paths (paper-fill insertion, strategy decide loop, metric computation, backtest replay) each have dedicated tests.
all_rules.rationale: The KB documents `pytest --cov=backend --cov-fail-under=80` as the canonical gate; these domains are the load-bearing logic of the system.

all_rules.rule: Place shared fixtures in conftest.py at the appropriate directory level; remember the closest conftest.py wins and can shadow higher-scope fixtures.
all_rules.rationale: The KB calls out conftest shadowing as a gotcha; placing fixtures thoughtfully avoids accidental overrides.

all_rules.rule: Configure Vitest with environment="jsdom", globals=true, setupFiles=["./vitest.setup.ts"], include=["**/*.{test,spec}.{ts,tsx}"], exclude=["**/node_modules/**", "**/e2e/**"], and coverage thresholds lines: 70.
all_rules.rationale: Excluding e2e keeps Playwright specs out of the Vitest runner; the KB's config is the documented baseline.

all_rules.rule: In vitest.setup.ts import "@testing-library/jest-dom/vitest" (not the non-vitest entry) and register afterEach(cleanup) from @testing-library/react.
all_rules.rationale: Wrong jest-dom entry silently omits matchers; missing cleanup leaks DOM between component tests.

all_rules.rule: Write React component tests with @testing-library/react using role-based queries (getByRole, getByLabel) and userEvent for interactions; assert visible text/state, not implementation details.
all_rules.rationale: The MarketCard and PlatformSelect examples query by role and visible text, which mirrors real user behavior and survives refactors.

all_rules.rule: Do not attempt to render React Server Components through Testing Library; extract logic into pure functions or Client Components for unit testing.
all_rules.rationale: RSCs are not real React components at runtime and cannot be rendered by RTL; the KB lists this as a gotcha.

all_rules.rule: Mock fetch in component/unit tests with vi.stubGlobal("fetch", ...); when using vi.mock(...), remember it is hoisted and use vi.hoisted() if the factory must reference outer variables.
all_rules.rationale: vi.mock hoisting is a documented footgun; stubGlobal is the KB's recommended pattern for fetch.

all_rules.rule: For time-dependent component logic (debounce, polling) use vi.useFakeTimers() + vi.advanceTimersByTime(...) and always restore with vi.useRealTimers() at the end.
all_rules.rationale: Fake timers are the deterministic way to test debounced UI; forgetting to restore leaks timers across tests.

all_rules.rule: Run Playwright E2E with a dual webServer array starting both Next.js (`pnpm dev` on 3000) and FastAPI (`uv run uvicorn backend.app.main:app --port 8000`), each with reuseExistingServer: !process.env.CI and timeout 120_000.
all_rules.rationale: E2E tests will hit a dead backend if only Next is started; the dual webServer pattern is the KB's prescribed setup.

all_rules.rule: Playwright config must set baseURL="http://localhost:3000", retries: process.env.CI ? 2 : 0, trace: "on-first-retry", screenshot: "only-on-failure", and declare projects for chromium and firefox.
all_rules.rationale: These settings give CI retry-on-flake with trace artifacts for debugging while keeping local runs fast and deterministic.

all_rules.rule: Never call page.waitForTimeout or arbitrary sleeps in Playwright; rely on built-in auto-waiting via waitForSelector and expect(...).toBeVisible/toHaveURL/toContainText.
all_rules.rationale: The KB explicitly flags waitForTimeout as flaky; auto-waiting assertions are deterministic.

all_rules.rule: Seed backend state in E2E via page.request or direct API calls, not page.evaluate, and mock backend failures with page.route("**/api/...", route => route.fulfill({ status, body })) to exercise error UI paths.
all_rules.rationale: page.evaluate for setup is brittle; route.fulfill is the KB-recommended way to simulate 502s and verify error surfaces.

all_rules.rule: Name tests descriptively in given-when-then style (e.g., "filter by kalshi", "handles backend 502", "test_list_markets_empty") so failure output reads as a sentence.
all_rules.rationale: The KB's examples consistently use descriptive, behavior-oriented names; this keeps CI logs interpretable without opening the code.

all_rules.rule: App shell MUST be a two-column CSS grid with grid-template-columns: 200px 1fr at widths >900px; the 200px left column is the sidebar, the 1fr right column is the main content area.
all_rules.rationale: wireframes.html .app sets grid-template-columns: 200px 1fr; every screen reuses this exact layout.

all_rules.rule: At viewport <=900px the sidebar MUST collapse to a 64px icon rail (grid-template-columns: 64px 1fr) and the .brand text label, .nav-list span.label text, and .nav-list .badge MUST all be hidden (display:none).
all_rules.rationale: wireframes.html @media (max-width: 900px) sets .app grid-template-columns: 64px 1fr and hides .brand span, .nav-list li span.label, .nav-list li .badge.

all_rules.rule: The sidebar nav MUST render exactly these 9 items in this exact order: Dashboard, Markets, Strategies, Paper Trades, Backtest, Compare, Portfolio, Data ingestion. No additional nav items may be inserted and none may be reordered.
all_rules.rationale: Every <ul class="nav-list"> block in wireframes.html lists these 8 li entries (plus an implicit 'current screen' as active); screen-inventory.json enumerates 9 canonical screens (Dashboard is the first).

all_rules.rule: The active nav item MUST be marked with class 'active' which sets background var(--bg-elevated) and MUST render a 2px-wide accent-colored vertical bar (::before pseudo-element) along the item's left edge inset 8px from top and bottom.
all_rules.rationale: wireframes.html .nav-list li.active and .nav-list li.active::before define this indicator on every screen's sidebar.

all_rules.rule: Nav items MAY display a numeric badge (e.g. Markets count 4218, Strategies count 3) rendered as an accent-subtle pill with mono font; badge element uses class 'badge' and MUST be hidden when sidebar is collapsed at <=900px.
all_rules.rationale: wireframes.html nav badges appear on Markets and Strategies only; .nav-list li .badge styling uses var(--accent-subtle) and var(--mono) and is hidden in the @media block.

all_rules.rule: The dashboard tile row MUST render 4 metric tiles (Equity, Brier score, Sharpe, Win rate) in a CSS grid with grid-template-columns: repeat(4, 1fr) at widths >900px. Each tile MUST render three stacked elements in order: .k (uppercase overline label), .v (mono value, 20px), .d (mono detail line).
all_rules.rationale: wireframes.html .tiles selector uses repeat(4, 1fr) and each .tile contains .k/.v/.d children; dashboard-elements.json confirms the four metrics.

all_rules.rule: At viewport <=900px, .tiles, .grid-2, and .grid-3 MUST reflow to 2-column layout (grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr). Do not hide tiles or collapse to single-column at this breakpoint.
all_rules.rationale: wireframes.html @media (max-width: 900px) overrides these three grid containers to two columns.

all_rules.rule: Status pills MUST use class 'pill' plus one of four state modifiers: .on (success green), .off (neutral grey), .warn (amber), .danger (red). Each pill MUST render a leading 5x5px colored dot (::before pseudo-element) unless the additional .plain class is applied.
all_rules.rationale: wireframes.html .pill.on/.off/.warn/.danger map to --success/--neutral-pill/--warning/--danger tokens; .pill::before renders the status dot and .pill.plain disables it.

all_rules.rule: Platform identity MUST be rendered as a .chip element with a platform-specific left-inset border (box-shadow inset 2px 0 0 0): Kalshi uses accent cyan, Polymarket uses #a78bfa purple, Manifold uses warning amber, PredictIt uses success green. Chip text is uppercase 3-letter abbreviation (KAL, POLY, MAN, PRE).
all_rules.rationale: wireframes.html .chip.kalshi/.polymarket/.manifold/.predictit each declare the inset box-shadow accent; all markets/trades/ingestion rows use the 3-letter abbreviation.

all_rules.rule: Trade/order side MUST be displayed with a .side-pill element using mono font, uppercase text: .side-pill.yes uses success-subtle background + success text, .side-pill.no uses danger-subtle background + danger text. Labels are 'BUY YES', 'SELL YES', 'BUY NO', or 'SELL NO'.
all_rules.rationale: wireframes.html .side-pill.yes/.no styles and the dashboard/paper-trades/market-detail rows exclusively use these four label variants.

all_rules.rule: Numeric table columns MUST carry the class 'num' which applies font-family var(--mono), text-align: right, and font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums. Apply to BOTH the <th> and every <td> in that column.
all_rules.rationale: wireframes.html td.num, th.num rule covers YES/NO price, Vol 24h, Close, Qty, Entry, Exit, P&L, Markets, Rows columns across all tables.

all_rules.rule: Table headers MUST use font-size 11px, color var(--fg-mute), font-weight 510, text-transform: uppercase, letter-spacing 0.08em, and a single-border-bottom with var(--border). Do not style <th> with regular body text weight or case.
all_rules.rationale: wireframes.html th selector defines this explicit header typography; every screen's data table follows it.

all_rules.rule: On the Markets screen, every data row MUST carry class 'link' and cursor: pointer so the entire row is clickable to open the market-detail screen. Non-link tables on other screens MUST omit the .link class.
all_rules.rationale: wireframes.html tr.link is applied only inside the #wf-markets table; markets-elements.json navigation_paths defines 'click market row -> market-detail'.

all_rules.rule: All tables MUST apply a subtle row-hover background (var(--bg-overlay)) via tr:hover td. Tables MUST NOT have zebra striping; separators are 1px bottom borders using var(--border-subtle), removed on the last row.
all_rules.rationale: wireframes.html tr:hover td, th/td border-bottom, and tr:last-child td { border-bottom: none } define this uniformly.

all_rules.rule: Filter bars MUST be a flex row (.filter-bar) with gap 8px, flex-wrap enabled, and MUST use a `` spacer to push action buttons (Export CSV, Export JSON, Run) to the right edge. Filter toggles use .pill.accent.plain for the selected state and .pill.off for unselected.
all_rules.rationale: wireframes.html .filter-bar selector and the Markets + Paper-Trades filter bars both use the flex:1 spacer pattern and the pill.accent.plain / pill.off toggle convention.

all_rules.rule: The Markets filter bar MUST include these controls in this order: text search input (min-width 240px), 'All' pill, one pill per platform (Kalshi/Polymarket/Manifold/PredictIt) showing current count, right-spacer, 'Open only' pill, 'Volume >$10k' pill.
all_rules.rationale: wireframes.html #wf-markets .filter-bar renders exactly this sequence; markets-elements.json confirms platform-filter-pill, search-input, open-only-filter, and volume-threshold-filter (threshold 10000).

all_rules.rule: The Paper-Trades filter bar MUST contain status pills (All/Open/Closed), a 1px vertical divider, strategy pills (Kelly/Arb/Momentum/Fade/MM/Manual), a right-spacer, and two ghost-style action buttons (Export CSV, Export JSON).
all_rules.rationale: wireframes.html #wf-paper-trades .filter-bar contains these exact groups; paper-trades-elements.json confirms filter-status, filter-strategy, export-csv, export-json.

all_rules.rule: Equity/performance curve charts MUST use a line (stroke-width 1.6) plus an area gradient fill (linearGradient top stop-opacity ~0.18-0.22, bottom stop-opacity 0), colored cyan (#7dd3fc) for live equity and green (#34d399) for backtest output. Do not render equity curves as bar charts or as plain lines without the gradient fill.
all_rules.rationale: wireframes.html Dashboard equity-curve uses #gEq (cyan gradient) + cyan polyline; Backtest equity-curve uses #gBt (green gradient) + green polyline; both follow the same pattern.

all_rules.rule: The Compare screen overlay chart MUST render multiple polyline series simultaneously, each in a distinct color drawn from the token set (cyan #7dd3fc, warning #fbbf24, danger #f87171 at minimum). Each row in the comparison table MUST display a 8x8px circle indicator in the row's series color as a legend.
all_rules.rationale: wireframes.html #wf-compare SVG shows three polylines in these three token colors and each comparison-table row prefixes the run-label with an 8x8 bg-colored circle.

all_rules.rule: The Portfolio P&L-attribution chart MUST render as a row of vertical stacked bars, one bar per strategy, with height proportional to |contribution|. Bars MUST color-code sign: positive contributions use green gradient (#34d399 -> #059669), negative contributions use red gradient (#f87171 -> #b91c1c), mixed/amber contributions use warning gradient (#fbbf24 -> #d97706).
all_rules.rationale: wireframes.html #wf-portfolio attribution chart renders inline div bars with exactly these three gradient stops; portfolio-elements.json confirms pnl-attribution-bars with bars-per-strategy.

all_rules.rule: Chart wells MUST use class 'chart-well' which is var(--bg-elevated) background, 1px border, radius-lg, padding 10px, and MUST have a defined height (default 180px; add .tall modifier for 240px). Never render a chart inside an unsized container.
all_rules.rationale: wireframes.html .chart-well sets height: 180px; .chart-well.tall overrides to 240px; recharts rule FE-R-022 already flags the unsized-parent bug.

all_rules.rule: The Dashboard MUST render, in top-to-bottom order: (1) head with h2 'Portfolio overview' + sub-line, (2) 4-tile metrics row, (3) tall equity curve chart-well, (4) grid-2 with 'Active strategies' table on the left and 'Recent paper trades' table on the right.
all_rules.rationale: wireframes.html #wf-dashboard main section follows exactly this vertical stack; dashboard-elements.json enumerates these 7 data elements.

all_rules.rule: The Market-Detail screen MUST render a grid-2: left cell contains the 90-day YES price chart (tall chart-well) with an overline caption showing 'current / high / low' values; right cell contains the paper-trade ticket card.inset with BUY-YES and BUY-NO buttons, size input, 'Cost / max loss' readout, and a full-width primary 'Submit paper trade' button.
all_rules.rationale: wireframes.html #wf-market-detail grid-2 block; market-detail-elements.json confirms buy-yes/buy-no, size-input, submit-paper-trade, cost and max-loss computed outputs.

all_rules.rule: The Strategies screen MUST render a grid-2 of strategy cards (one per registered strategy) plus a final dashed-border 'drop-zone' card instructing users to 'Drop a .py file in strategies/ to register a new plugin'. Card count is 5 strategies + 1 drop zone = 6 cells.
all_rules.rationale: wireframes.html #wf-strategies grid-2 contains 5 .card.inset entries plus a trailing dashed-border card; strategies-elements.json lists five strategy cards (kelly/arb/momentum/news-fade/market-maker).

all_rules.rule: Each strategy card MUST contain a card-head row with h3 name + on/off pill, a one-line dim description paragraph (font-size 12px), and a single mono line showing key parameters + 'P&L <value>'. P&L value uses .pos/.neg helper classes; show '—' when strategy is off or has zero trades.
all_rules.rationale: wireframes.html every #wf-strategies .card.inset follows this exact three-part template; strategies-elements.json card_contents lists these same fields.

all_rules.rule: The Backtest screen MUST render a grid-2 where the left cell is a configuration card.inset (Strategy dropdown, Platforms chip row, Date range input, Starting bankroll input, primary 'Run backtest' button) and the right cell contains a 2x2 results-tiles grid followed by the equity-curve chart-well.tall and a 'Save as comparison' ghost button.
all_rules.rationale: wireframes.html #wf-backtest grid-2 structure + backtest-elements.json listing strategy-picker, platform-multiselect, date-range-picker, starting-bankroll, run-backtest-button, save-as-comparison-button, 4 tiles, and equity-curve.

all_rules.rule: The Data-Ingestion screen MUST render one table row per adapter (Kalshi, Polymarket, Manifold, PredictIt) with columns: Platform (chip + name), Status pill, Last sync (mono dim timestamp), Markets count (num), Rows count (num, allow 'M' suffix for millions), Action button. Status 'healthy' uses .pill.on 'HEALTHY'; 'rate-limited' uses .pill.warn 'RATE LIMITED'.
all_rules.rationale: wireframes.html #wf-data-ingestion table has exactly these 4 rows and 6 columns; data-ingestion-elements.json defines status_values and rows.

all_rules.rule: The Data-Ingestion screen MUST render a dim footnote paragraph below the adapters table explaining that PredictIt data is recorded live only (no historical API) and that backtests on PredictIt can only cover dates from first live-sync onward.
all_rules.rationale:
wireframes.html #wf-data-ingestion ends with a

containing this exact note; it encodes a real data-availability constraint that users must see.

all_rules.rule: Action buttons MUST use .btn with exactly one variant class: .btn.primary (accent background, dark text), .btn.ghost (overlay background, bordered), or .btn.danger (danger-subtle background, danger text + border). The Submit paper trade and Run backtest buttons MUST be .btn.primary with width:100% inside their forms.
all_rules.rationale: wireframes.html .btn.primary/.ghost/.danger definitions; submit-paper-trade and Run backtest buttons use style="width:100%".

all_rules.rule: Screen headers (.screen-header) MUST render a 2-digit zero-padded ordinal (01-09) in accent mono 12px, followed by the screen title in sans 15px 600-weight, and a right-aligned URL pill in mono 11px showing the route (e.g. 'localhost:3000/markets'). The ordinals and titles are canonical: 01 Dashboard, 02 Markets, 03 Market detail, 04 Strategies, 05 Paper Trades, 06 Backtest, 07 Compare, 08 Portfolio, 09 Data ingestion.
all_rules.rationale: wireframes.html every .screen-header block follows exactly this structure with these ordinals and titles; they align 1:1 with screen-inventory.json.

all_rules.rule: Positive monetary/percentage values MUST use class 'pos' (color var(--success)); negative values MUST use class 'neg' (color var(--danger)); inactive/empty values MUST use class 'dim' (color var(--fg-mute)) with a literal em-dash '—' glyph. Do not color positive values blue or negative values orange.
all_rules.rationale: wireframes.html .pos/.neg/.dim utility classes; all P&L cells across dashboard/paper-trades/compare/portfolio use these consistently and show '—' when unavailable.

all_rules.rule: Timestamps in tables MUST be rendered with class 'mono dim' showing HH:MM 24-hour format (e.g. '14:02') for intraday rows, and HH:MM:SS for precise sync timestamps (Data-Ingestion last-sync). Do not use 12-hour AM/PM or localized date strings inside these tables.
all_rules.rationale: wireframes.html dashboard and paper-trades tables all use 14:02 style; data-ingestion uses HH:MM:SS (14:04:02) for Last sync.

all_rules.rule: Body root MUST set font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums globally so every digit across the app has fixed width, and the .mono utility + <code> MUST additionally apply the var(--mono) monospace family with tabular-nums. Prices, counts, timestamps, P&L, and durations MUST never render with proportional-width digits.
all_rules.rationale: wireframes.html body selector sets font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums; .mono rule also applies it. This aligns with FE-R-019 (tailwind mono/tabular-nums) but is enforced at the app-shell level here.

all_rules.rule: Define all color tokens in the @theme block of app/globals.css. Never hardcode hex values (e.g. #7dd3fc, #34d399, #f87171) in className strings, inline styles, or chart props — always reference var(--accent), var(--success), var(--danger), etc.
all_rules.rationale: Centralized tokens keep Tailwind utilities, Recharts components, and custom CSS visually in sync; hardcoded hex silently drifts from the theme when tokens change.

all_rules.rule: Use --accent (#7dd3fc sky-cyan) ONLY for interactive chrome: primary CTAs, links, active nav items, focus rings, sort indicators, and the default chart series stroke. Never use --accent for body text, decorative accents, or non-interactive labels.
all_rules.rationale: Single chromatic accent on achromatic surfaces is Linear's core pattern. Using accent on non-interactive elements destroys its affordance signal and drowns out real CTAs.

all_rules.rule: Gain/positive P&L/BUY YES MUST use --success (#34d399) for foreground text and --success-subtle (rgba(52,211,153,0.14)) for badge backgrounds. Loss/negative P&L/BUY NO MUST use --danger (#f87171) / --danger-subtle. Never substitute generic green/red hex values.
all_rules.rationale: Unambiguous P&L signaling is non-negotiable in a trading UI. Token-enforced semantic mapping prevents an inconsistent red/green palette across tables, tiles, pills, and charts.

all_rules.rule: Warning/caution states (rate-limited adapters, fade signals, news-spike alerts) MUST use --warning (#fbbf24) / --warning-subtle — not --accent and not --danger.
all_rules.rationale: Amber encodes a distinct degraded-but-not-broken state; confusing warning with error (danger) or with info (accent) leads operators to misread adapter health at a glance.

all_rules.rule: Page background MUST be --bg (#0b0f17). Sidebar and docked panels MUST be --bg-panel (#0f1011). Card surfaces MUST be --bg-surface (#111826). Tiles, tooltips, and hover-elevated cards MUST be --bg-elevated (#182236). These tiers are ordinal — never elevate a tile onto the sidebar surface or drop a card below the page background.
all_rules.rationale: Linear communicates elevation through luminance stepping on dark surfaces. Violating the tier order breaks the visual depth model and makes nested surfaces unreadable.

all_rules.rule: Borders MUST use semi-transparent white tokens: --border (rgba(255,255,255,0.08)) for default cards/inputs/rows, --border-subtle (rgba(255,255,255,0.05)) for list/row dividers, --border-strong (#1f2a40) for sidebar/panel separators, --border-accent (#2b3a58) for focused inputs. Do not invent custom border colors.
all_rules.rationale: Semi-transparent borders adapt to underlying surface luminance; opaque custom borders visibly break when the surface beneath changes.

all_rules.rule: Text color MUST be selected from the five-step foreground ramp: --fg for primary, --fg-secondary for long-form body, --fg-dim for metadata/labels, --fg-mute for placeholders/overlines, --fg-disabled for disabled states. Never use raw white (#fff) or arbitrary gray hex values for text.
all_rules.rationale: The ramp is tuned for cool-cast readability on the dark surface tiers; skipping levels produces inconsistent contrast and breaks the hierarchy between primary content and chrome.

all_rules.rule: Body, UI chrome, nav, buttons, and headings MUST use the Inter stack: Inter Variable, Inter, -apple-system, Segoe UI, Roboto, system-ui, sans-serif. Do not apply Inter to numeric data cells.
all_rules.rationale: Inter is the designated proportional face; mixing other sans fonts breaks vertical rhythm, and applying Inter to numbers loses tabular alignment.

all_rules.rule: All prices, percentages, P&L values, Brier scores, Sharpe ratios, quantities, timestamps, tickers, and code snippets MUST be set in the Mono stack: Berkeley Mono, JetBrains Mono, ui-monospace, SFMono-Regular, Menlo, Consolas, monospace. JetBrains Mono is the required free fallback when Berkeley Mono is unlicensed.
all_rules.rationale: Mono stack with stable glyph widths is required for columnar alignment; fallback ordering guarantees a licensed face is always available without shipping the paid primary.

all_rules.rule: Every numeric cell or value MUST have font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums applied (via font-mono utility, tnum OpenType feature, or direct CSS). The global stylesheet must include "cv01", "ss03", "tnum" in font-feature-settings so digit widths lock by default.
all_rules.rationale: Proportional digits cause visible horizontal jitter every tick update; tabular-nums is mandatory for readable, non-juddering price columns and P&L streams.

all_rules.rule: Use weight 510 as the workhorse medium for UI labels, nav items, button text, pills, captions, and overlines. Reserve 400 for body prose, 500 for mono values, 600 for H1-H3 headings, 650 for display.
all_rules.rationale: Weight 510 is Linear's variable-font trick for optical medium that sits between regular and semibold — it disambiguates labels from body without the heaviness of 600 on small UI.

all_rules.rule: Overline / micro-label text (tile labels, section overlines) MUST be Inter 11px / weight 510 / uppercase / letter-spacing 0.08em / color var(--fg-mute). Never apply uppercase to body, button, or nav text.
all_rules.rationale: The overline is the only place uppercase is permitted; using caps elsewhere breaks Linear's quiet hierarchy and reduces legibility on data-dense screens.

all_rules.rule: Typographic scale is fixed: Display 30/650, H1 24/600, H2 20/600, H3 16/600, Body 14/400, Body Medium 14/510, Small 13/400, Caption 12/510, Micro 11/510, Tile Value mono 20/500, Table Cell mono 13/400, Price Big mono 24/500, Code mono 12/400. Do not introduce off-scale sizes or weights.
all_rules.rationale: A locked scale is what keeps a data-dense dashboard calm; arbitrary sizes (e.g. 15px, 17px) visibly misalign grids and table headers.

all_rules.rule: Spacing MUST use the 4px-based token scale: --space-1 4px, --space-2 8px, --space-3 12px, --space-4 16px, --space-5 20px, --space-6 24px, --space-8 32px, --space-10 40px, --space-12 48px, --space-16 64px. The dominant rhythm is 4 → 8 → 16 → 24 → 32. Never use arbitrary pixel values (e.g. 5px, 7px, 18px) for padding or margin.
all_rules.rationale: Every surface aligns to a 4px grid; off-grid spacing produces sub-pixel drift that accumulates across a multi-panel dashboard.

all_rules.rule: Table body rows (td) MUST cap vertical padding at 12px (padding 10px 8px is the reference default). Never exceed 12px Y-padding on dense data tables (Markets, Paper Trades, Portfolio, Compare).
all_rules.rationale: Dense trading tables must show 20+ rows without scrolling; exceeding 12px Y-padding pushes below-the-fold content off-screen on standard laptop viewports.

all_rules.rule: Default card padding is --space-5 (20px); compact tile padding is 12px 16px; table header/body padding is 10px 8px; button padding is 8px 14px; icon button padding is 6px; input padding is 8px 10px; pill/badge padding is 2px 8px (or 2px 10px for adapter status).
all_rules.rationale: Component-specific padding constants are what give Linear its tight but breathable feel; ad-hoc padding produces visually inconsistent component widths and heights across screens.

all_rules.rule: Border radius MUST be selected from the token scale: --radius-xs 2px (inline badges, dots), --radius-sm 4px (small chips, platform chips, side pills), --radius-md 6px (buttons, inputs, toolbar items, tooltips), --radius-lg 8px (cards default, tiles, dropdowns, chart containers), --radius-xl 12px (featured cards, hero panels), --radius-2xl 14px (page-level screen frames), --radius-full 9999px (pills, status chips, adapter status), --radius-circle 50% (icon buttons, avatars, dots).
all_rules.rationale: A fixed radius scale per component role is what makes nested surfaces read correctly; random radii make cards-inside-cards look misaligned.

all_rules.rule: Elevation on dark surfaces MUST be communicated by background luminance stepping (bg-panel → bg-surface → bg-elevated), NOT by drop shadows. Reserve --shadow-md and --shadow-lg strictly for floating/overlay surfaces: dropdowns, popovers, modals, command palette, tooltips.
all_rules.rationale: Drop shadows on flat cards create a noisy, glowing look on dark UI; luminance stepping is the native dark-mode technique and reads as depth without visual clutter.

all_rules.rule: Cards MUST default to --shadow-none. Apply --shadow-sm (border-as-shadow ring) only on hover for clickable cards. Dropdowns and hover-elevated cards use --shadow-md. Modals, command palette, and popovers use --shadow-lg. Chart wells use --shadow-inset.
all_rules.rationale: Shadow-per-role is load-bearing for the depth model; mis-applied shadows either flatten hierarchy (modal with --shadow-sm) or noise up static layouts (card with --shadow-lg).

all_rules.rule: Keyboard focus rings on all interactive elements (buttons, inputs, links, nav items, icon buttons) MUST be --shadow-focus (0 0 0 2px rgba(125,211,252,0.35)). Do not use default browser outlines and do not substitute a solid border for the ring.
all_rules.rationale: Accessible focus signaling is a hard requirement; a single consistent cyan ring also aligns with keyboard-first Linear navigation patterns.

all_rules.rule: Button variants are exactly four: Primary (bg --accent, text #0b0f17, radius --radius-md, Inter 14/510, padding 8px 14px), Ghost/Secondary (bg --bg-overlay, text --fg, border 1px --border, same sizing), Destructive (bg --danger-subtle, text --danger, border 1px rgba(248,113,113,0.24), same sizing), Icon (transparent bg, padding 6px, radius --radius-md, color --fg-dim hover --fg). Do not create additional button variants.
all_rules.rationale: A bounded variant set keeps the CTA hierarchy legible; bespoke buttons (outline-accent, filled-success-button, etc.) erode the primary-vs-ghost distinction.

all_rules.rule: Primary button text color MUST be #0b0f17 (explicit dark, matching --bg) on the cyan --accent background — NOT --fg and NOT white. This is the only place a hardcoded hex is permitted because the cyan requires a specific dark foreground for WCAG contrast.
all_rules.rationale: #7dd3fc cyan on white text fails contrast; cyan on near-black (#0b0f17) passes AA and is the only legible primary CTA combination.

all_rules.rule: Card component MUST be: bg --bg-surface, border 1px --border, radius --radius-xl (12px), padding --space-5 (20px), shadow --shadow-none default / --shadow-sm on hover-if-clickable, header uses H3 (Inter 16/600) with optional overline micro-label in --fg-mute.
all_rules.rationale: Card is the default container; pinning its exact spec keeps every dashboard screen (Markets, Strategies, Backtest, etc.) visually homogeneous.

all_rules.rule: Tile component (metric tile for equity, Brier, Sharpe, win rate) is distinct from Card: bg --bg-elevated (NOT --bg-surface), radius --radius-lg (8px, NOT --radius-xl), padding 12px 16px. Structure is three stacked rows: overline Label (Inter 11/510 uppercase --fg-mute) → Value (Mono 20/500 --fg tabular-nums) → optional Delta (Mono 11, --success if positive / --danger if negative). Grid is repeat(4, 1fr) desktop, repeat(2, 1fr) ≤700px.
all_rules.rationale: Tiles and Cards serve different roles — tiles show one metric, cards contain mixed content. Collapsing them visually (e.g. using --bg-surface on a tile) makes the dashboard read as a wall of identical boxes.

all_rules.rule: Table MUST use border-collapse: collapse, width 100%, header th in Inter 11/510 uppercase 0.08em tracking color --fg-mute with border-bottom 1px --border, body td padding 10px 8px with border-bottom 1px --border-subtle, last row no border-bottom, row hover bg rgba(255,255,255,0.02). No zebra striping — rely on the whisper-thin --border-subtle per row.
all_rules.rationale: Zebra striping fights with the dark luminance-stepping model and breaks when rows highlight; whisper dividers scale to any row count without visual noise.

all_rules.rule: Numeric table columns MUST be right-aligned and use font-mono + tabular-nums. Use semantic classes: .pos → color --success, .neg → color --danger, .dim → color --fg-mute. Sort indicator on the active sorted header is a small caret in --accent.
all_rules.rationale: Right-aligned, tabular-nums, tokenized semantic classes are what make a long P&L column readable at a glance; left-aligned or proportional digits make scanning impossible.

all_rules.rule: Sidebar MUST be width 180px (collapsible to 56px icon-only), bg --bg-panel, border-right 1px --border-strong, padding 14px 12px. Nav li uses Inter 12-13px weight 510, color --fg-dim, padding 7px 8px, radius --radius-md. Hover sets bg --bg-overlay-hover / color --fg. Active sets bg --bg-elevated / color --fg with an optional 2px left --accent bar flush with the item. Nav list is exactly 8 items: Dashboard, Markets, Strategies, Paper Trades, Backtest, Compare, Portfolio, Data Ingestion.
all_rules.rationale: Sidebar content is the top-level navigation contract; adding or removing items without updating screen-inventory.json breaks the routing contract.

all_rules.rule: Sidebar badges (count, status) MUST be text 10px color --accent, bg --accent-subtle, radius 10px, padding 1px 6px. Do not use --success-subtle or --danger-subtle for sidebar nav badges — those are reserved for pill components.
all_rules.rationale: Nav badges are informational counts, not status signals; using trading-semantic colors on nav creates false P&L associations.

all_rules.rule: Chart container MUST be: bg --bg-elevated, border 1px --border, radius --radius-lg (8px), padding 10px. Inner grid lines use --border-subtle. Axis labels are mono 11px --fg-mute tabular-nums. Default series stroke is --accent with a linear-gradient fill from rgba(125,211,252,0.18) to transparent. Positive regions fill --success, negative regions fill --danger. Multi-series (Compare) palette is fixed: cyan → emerald → amber → #a78bfa violet → #f472b6 pink (max 5 runs).
all_rules.rationale: Chart theme must mirror UI tokens exactly (via chartTheme.ts) so a positive equity curve reads the same green as a positive P&L cell; divergence between Recharts and CSS tokens breaks the visual grammar.

all_rules.rule: Chart tooltip MUST be: bg --bg-elevated, border 1px --border, radius --radius-md, padding 8px 10px, mono 12px, shadow --shadow-md. Tooltip styling must be applied via Recharts contentStyle (inline), never via className.
all_rules.rationale: Recharts tooltip className styling is unreliable (documented gotcha); inline contentStyle is the only supported customization path and keeps the tooltip token-aligned.

all_rules.rule: Input component MUST be: bg --bg-overlay, text --fg Inter 14/400, placeholder --fg-mute, border 1px --border, radius --radius-md, padding 8px 10px. Focus MUST set border to --accent-border AND apply --shadow-focus. Disabled state uses color --fg-disabled and bg rgba(255,255,255,0.01). Numeric inputs (size, price, bankroll) MUST use mono + tabular-nums and be right-aligned.
all_rules.rationale: Input focus state needs both a color and a ring because the semi-transparent border alone is invisible at a glance; numeric inputs must align with their display counterparts in tables/tiles.

all_rules.rule: Side pill (BUY YES / BUY NO / SELL YES / SELL NO) MUST render: YES variant with bg --success-subtle, text --success; NO variant with bg --danger-subtle, text --danger. Padding 2px 8px, radius --radius-sm, font mono 11px weight 500 uppercase. Never invert YES/NO colors or use --accent for side pills.
all_rules.rationale: YES=success / NO=danger mapping is the trading UI's most load-bearing semantic. Inverting them would actively mislead traders placing paper orders.

all_rules.rule: Adapter status pill MUST render exactly three variants with a leading dot ●: HEALTHY (bg --success-subtle, text --success), RATE LIMITED (bg --warning-subtle, text --warning), ERROR (bg --danger-subtle, text --danger). Padding 2px 10px, radius --radius-full, font Inter 11/510 uppercase letter-spacing 0.04em. Do not add additional status states without updating this rule.
all_rules.rationale: Adapter health is a ternary state surfaced on the Data Ingestion screen; the three-variant contract must match the backend's adapter-health enum exactly.

all_rules.rule: Strategy status pill (on/off) MUST be: On — bg --success-subtle, text --success, filled dot ● in --success; Off — bg --neutral-pill, text --fg-dim, hollow dot ○ in --fg-mute. Padding 2px 8px, radius --radius-full, font Inter 11/510.
all_rules.rationale: On/off is binary but shares the health ternary's visual grammar; using --neutral-pill (not --danger) for Off avoids encoding an idle strategy as an error.

all_rules.rule: Platform chip (Kalshi / Polymarket / Manifold / PredictIt) MUST be: bg --neutral-pill (rgba(142,160,189,0.12)), text --fg, padding 2px 8px, radius --radius-sm (4px), font Inter 11/510. Per-platform accent tint is applied as a subtle left border or dot only: Kalshi → cyan (--accent), Polymarket → violet (#a78bfa), Manifold → amber (--warning), PredictIt → emerald (--success). Never fill the chip background with the platform-accent color.
all_rules.rationale: Platform chips must remain visually quiet because multiple can appear in one row; filled-background platform chips would compete with the P&L and side semantic colors.

all_rules.rule: Transitions MUST be 150ms ease-out for hover, 200ms ease-out for focus, 250ms ease-in-out for expand/collapse (including sidebar collapse). Modal backdrop fades 150ms; modal dialog scales 0.98 → 1 in 200ms. Do not invent ad-hoc timings (e.g. 300ms, 180ms).
all_rules.rationale: A three-step timing system keeps the UI feeling responsive without jitter; inconsistent durations produce a laggy feel on data-dense screens.

all_rules.rule: NEVER animate color transitions on financial data during refresh (no red/green flashing of P&L or price cells). The ONLY permitted update animation on streaming rows is an opacity pulse 0.4 → 1 over 600ms on newly inserted rows.
all_rules.rationale: Color flashes on every tick is visually exhausting and obscures real direction changes; opacity pulse on insertion signals 'new' without introducing false movement signals.

all_rules.rule: The app MUST be dark-only. Set color-scheme: dark on :root, apply @variant dark (&:where(:root, :root *)); in globals.css to force dark utilities, and DO NOT ship a light-mode toggle, prefers-color-scheme override, or theme switcher.
all_rules.rationale: Deployment contract is dark-only (tech-stack.json deployment.theme); adding a theme switcher doubles the design-system surface area and was explicitly rejected.

all_rules.rule: When linking multiple Recharts charts on the same x-axis (price + volume on Market Detail, strategy comparison on Compare), give them the same syncId so hover crosshairs align. Event annotations (news spikes, resolution times) MUST use <ReferenceLine> / <ReferenceArea> on the existing chart — never a separate stacked chart.
all_rules.rationale: Synchronized hover is a core affordance for multi-series analysis; separate annotation charts force the user to mentally correlate across containers.

all_rules.rule: The sky-cyan accent (#7dd3fc) and trading-semantic trio (#34d399 / #f87171 / #fbbf24) are the documented deltas from Linear's base system. The --bg-surface (#111826) and --bg-elevated (#182236) values override Linear's #191a1b / #28282c to match the approved proposal palette. Any further palette change MUST update this design system file AND every consumer token map (chartTheme.ts, globals.css @theme block) in the same commit.
all_rules.rationale: Palette drift between design-system.md, globals.css, and chartTheme.ts is the single largest source of visual inconsistency; atomic updates across all three are mandatory.

all_rules.rule: Kalshi adapter MUST target base URL https://api.elections.kalshi.com/trade-api/v2 for production reads and https://demo-api.kalshi.co/trade-api/v2 for any smoke tests; base URL MUST come from config, never hardcoded in call sites.
all_rules.rationale: Feature-discoveries ADP-01 pins these exact base URLs; separation from demo is required so tests never accidentally hit production.

all_rules.rule: Kalshi adapter MUST operate in api-key-optional mode: when no KALSHI_API_KEY is present, issue unsigned reads against public endpoints only; when a key is present, RSA-PKCS#8-2048 sign every request with headers KALSHI-ACCESS-KEY, KALSHI-ACCESS-SIGNATURE, and KALSHI-ACCESS-TIMESTAMP.
all_rules.rationale: ADP-01 auth_model + pitfall: RSA signing is heavyweight and unnecessary for purely-read adapter; fall back to unsigned public reads.

all_rules.rule: Kalshi adapter MUST convert integer-cent prices (range 1-99) returned by /markets/{ticker}/orderbook into decimal market-price in [0.00, 1.00] by dividing by 100 before storage.
all_rules.rationale: ADP-01 pitfall: integer-cent wire format collides with disambiguated.txt decimal market-price; INV-002 requires 0 <= market.price <= 1.

all_rules.rule: Kalshi adapter MUST treat YES and NO as complementary: store only yes-price and derive no-price = 1 - yes-price. Do NOT emit an independent no-side order book.
all_rules.rationale: ADP-01 market-side_encoding: each market is paired binary; there is no independent NO order book on Kalshi.

all_rules.rule: Kalshi adapter MUST throttle reads with a token bucket at <= 20 req/sec (Basic tier) with a 10% safety margin (effective 18/sec). On any 429 response, honour the Retry-After header before retrying and surface adapter.status='rate-limited' on the data-ingestion screen.
all_rules.rationale: ADP-01 rate_limits Basic tier + throttle_behaviour + SR-003; FS-006 requires accurate status reporting.

all_rules.rule: Kalshi adapter MUST compute market-volume-24h by summing trades from the trades feed over the trailing 24h; do NOT rely on a direct 24h volume field on the ticker response.
all_rules.rationale: ADP-01 known_quirks: no direct 24h field is present on all Kalshi tickers.

all_rules.rule: Kalshi adapter MAY subscribe to public websocket channels (ticker, trade, orderbook_delta, market_lifecycle_v2) WITHOUT authentication; the websocket URL MUST be wss://api.elections.kalshi.com/trade-api/ws/v2.
all_rules.rationale: ADP-01 data_format and patterns_found: public channels need no auth.

all_rules.rule: Kalshi adapter MUST map the Kalshi status field to the unified market-resolution enum {yes-resolved, no-resolved, cancelled, open}. Unknown status values MUST raise AdapterError rather than being silently coerced to open.
all_rules.rationale: ADP-01 market-resolution_mapping; silent coercion masks resolution bugs that corrupt brier-score denominators.

all_rules.rule: Polymarket adapter MUST use three distinct base URLs: gamma-api.polymarket.com for market discovery (list_markets), clob.polymarket.com for order book + prices-history, data-api.polymarket.com for data endpoints. Each domain has its own rate-limit bucket and MUST be throttled independently.
all_rules.rationale: ADP-02 api_base_urls; mixing domains into one throttler either under- or over-rates the adapter.

all_rules.rule: Polymarket adapter MUST run in auth=none-for-read mode. It MUST NOT implement L2 HMAC-SHA256 signing and MUST NOT import the write-path portion of py-clob-client. Any code that constructs a POST body for a trading endpoint is forbidden.
all_rules.rationale: ADP-02 auth_model + INV-007 (no write paths to real trading endpoints).

all_rules.rule: Polymarket gamma discovery calls MUST be throttled at <= 60 requests/minute (conservative) until live metrics prove the 4000/10s upper figure. Configuration MUST expose the value so it can be widened without redeploy.
all_rules.rationale: ADP-02 rate_limits: sources disagree (60/min vs 4000/10s); start conservative per pitfall mitigation.

all_rules.rule: Polymarket CLOB websocket subscriptions MUST multiplex ALL markets through a single shared connection per IP; adapter MUST NOT open more than 1 CLOB websocket connection.
all_rules.rationale: ADP-02 rate_limits.websocket cap = 5 connections/IP; single-connection multiplexing is the documented mitigation.

all_rules.rule: Polymarket adapter MUST treat yes-token and no-token as two independent tokens with distinct token_ids and distinct order books. Adapter MUST resolve market_id -> tokens[] before requesting prices-history; price-history calls use token_id, not market_id.
all_rules.rationale: ADP-02 market-side_encoding + known_quirks: CLOB exposes two separate token IDs per market.

all_rules.rule: Polymarket adapter MUST persist both yes-token-price and no-token-price separately in stored-price-history and MUST NOT derive one from the other. Divergence (yes + no != 1.00) is valid input to STR-02 arbitrage detection.
