all_rules.rationale: ADP-02 pitfall: yes+no can diverge; forcing yes+no=1 destroys the arbitrage signal STR-02 depends on.

all_rules.rule: Polymarket adapter MUST flatten the gamma Series -> Events -> Markets hierarchy into flat market entities keyed by (platform, platform_market_id) before emitting ingest-markets events.
all_rules.rationale: ADP-02 known_quirks: gamma.markets returns event hierarchy; unified schema is flat per PR-004 idempotency requirement.

all_rules.rule: Polymarket adapter MUST filter discovered markets to those with enableOrderBook == true; markets without a live book are ui-priced only and cannot be traded in paper mode.
all_rules.rationale: ADP-02 known_quirks: enableOrderBook flag gates tradability; INV-009 slippage-model requires an order book.

all_rules.rule: Manifold adapter MUST default to unauthenticated reads; authenticated mode accepts either Authorization: Key {api_key} or Authorization: Bearer {jwt} headers but MUST NOT require them.
all_rules.rationale: ADP-03 auth_model: api-key-optional; read endpoints are public.

all_rules.rule: Manifold adapter MUST throttle at <= 500 req/min per IP with a safety margin (effective <= 450/min). On 429 apply exponential backoff.
all_rules.rationale: ADP-03 rate_limits.per_ip_per_minute = 500.

all_rules.rule: Manifold adapter MUST filter ingested markets to outcomeType == 'BINARY' for the v1 unified schema. Non-binary types (MULTIPLE_CHOICE, PSEUDO_NUMERIC, POLL, BOUNTIED_QUESTION, FREE_RESPONSE) MUST NOT be written to the markets table unless a future opt-in flag explicitly enables PSEUDO_NUMERIC mapping.
all_rules.rationale: ADP-03 pitfalls + OQ-10 recommended default: filter to binary only in v1.

all_rules.rule: Manifold adapter MUST reconstruct price-history by paging GET /v0/bets?contractId={id} and deriving each tick from probBefore/probAfter on each bet. First sync pages through the full bet history; subsequent syncs MUST be incremental using a createdTime cursor.
all_rules.rationale: ADP-03 price_history_endpoint = NOT AVAILABLE; reconstruction from bets is the documented pattern.

all_rules.rule: Manifold adapter MUST read the BINARY market's probability field directly as yes-price in [0.0, 1.0]; no-price is derived as 1 - yes-price (unlike Polymarket).
all_rules.rationale: ADP-03 market-side_encoding: single probability field per binary market.

all_rules.rule: Manifold adapter MUST keep market-volume-24h in native mana currency and MUST NOT auto-convert to USD at ingest; USD mapping happens only at paper-fill time via the simulated-bankroll model.
all_rules.rationale: ADP-03 known_quirks + pitfall: mana vs usd confusion; INV-001 usd-denominated equity is separate from mana-denominated volume.

all_rules.rule: Manifold websocket connections MUST send a keepalive ping every 30-60s; adapter MUST detect dropped connections and auto-reconnect with exponential backoff (base 1s, max 60s).
all_rules.rationale: ADP-03 known_quirks: websocket requires keepalive or connection drops.

all_rules.rule: PredictIt adapter MUST poll GET /all/ at <= 1 request/second and MUST NOT issue more frequent polls since upstream data itself refreshes only every 60 seconds.
all_rules.rationale: ADP-04 rate_limits + data_refresh: 1 req/sec documented + 60s upstream cache.

all_rules.rule: PredictIt adapter MUST be read-only. There is no trading API; any method that attempts to write or authenticate beyond public read is forbidden.
all_rules.rationale: ADP-04 auth_model: 'NO trading api at all'; INV-007.

all_rules.rule: PredictIt adapter MUST flatten multi-contract markets: each contract becomes its own normalised market in the unified schema, keyed by (platform='predictit', platform_market_id=f'{market_id}:{contract_id}').
all_rules.rationale: ADP-04 pitfall: multi-contract markets break the one-market/one-binary assumption; flatten per contract.

all_rules.rule: PredictIt adapter MUST verify per-field price units: bestBuyYesCost / bestBuyNoCost / bestSellYesCost / bestSellNoCost / lastTradePrice / lastClosePrice are quoted in cents in some fields; adapter MUST normalise each to decimal [0.0, 1.0] using a per-field unit table and reject prices outside [0.0, 1.0] after normalisation.
all_rules.rationale: ADP-04 known_quirks: 'prices are in cents not dollars in some fields - verify per-field'; FS-002 forbids out-of-range prices.

all_rules.rule: PredictIt backtest date-range UI MUST disable selection of dates earlier than min(stored-price-history.timestamp) for the PredictIt platform and render a tooltip reading 'No PredictIt data recorded before {date}'.
all_rules.rationale: ADP-04 historical_available = NO + OQ-01 recommended default.

all_rules.rule: Any UI surface that renders PredictIt prices or market-volume MUST display a PredictIt attribution badge (e.g. platform-chip reading 'PredictIt').
all_rules.rationale: ADP-04 known_quirks: non-commercial data-license requires attribution on any UI surface.

all_rules.rule: Every adapter's list_markets method MUST normalise to the shared schema using composite key (platform, platform_market_id); ingest-markets MUST be idempotent on conflict (UPSERT, not INSERT).
all_rules.rationale: PR-004 idempotency requirement + ADP-01..04 normalisation patterns.

all_rules.rule: No adapter method may POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE to a platform's trading endpoint. Every adapter method must be reviewable as HTTP GET only (or websocket read-subscribe only). CI MUST include a grep/AST check failing on httpx.post|httpx.put|httpx.patch|httpx.delete inside adapter packages.
all_rules.rationale: INV-007 boundary invariant B1; fail-fast static check is the only enforceable defence.

all_rules.rule: Adapter health transitions MUST follow the {healthy, rate-limited, error, syncing} enum. An adapter MUST NOT report healthy when now - last_sync > 10 * poll_interval; the background scheduler MUST flip status to 'error' in that case.
all_rules.rationale: FS-006 forbidden state + adapter entity lifecycle in semantic-spec.

all_rules.rule: Every adapter MUST raise AdapterError (mapped by the FastAPI handler to HTTP 502 with {platform, detail}) on upstream failures; adapters MUST NOT swallow upstream errors and return empty lists.
all_rules.rationale: BE-R-019 (backend rule) binding: 502 upstream-failure pattern; silent empty-list returns hide FS-006 conditions.

all_rules.rule: Event-driven backtest engine MUST NOT call adapter.stream_live_prices or any live-price method during execute-backtest; backtest data source MUST be stored-price-history only.
all_rules.rationale: INV-006 + ENG-01 simulation_vs_reality_line.

all_rules.rule: Event-driven backtest engine MUST emit market-data-events in strictly chronological order; strategy.decide(market-state) MUST be called with state at time t only and MUST NOT be handed any tick with ts > current event ts.
all_rules.rationale: ENG-01 pitfall: look-ahead bias prevention; strategy contract preservation.

all_rules.rule: Slippage-model MUST fill orders with size <= top-of-book size at mid ± 1 cent (buy above mid, sell below mid). For larger orders it MUST walk the order book level by level, consuming quantity at each price, and set fill-price to the volume-weighted average across consumed levels.
all_rules.rationale: ENG-01 slippage_model_implementation + order_book_crossing; INV-009 binds paper-fill.entry-price to slippage-model output.

all_rules.rule: Cross-platform-arbitrage (STR-02) leg 2 MUST be executed at the next event tick after leg 1 (not simultaneously). The engine re-reads platform-B market-price at t+poll_interval or next market-data-event before applying slippage-model to leg 2.
all_rules.rationale: STR-02 execution_sequencing_risk: fills are never simultaneous; realistic paper simulation requires deferred leg 2.

all_rules.rule: Cross-platform-arbitrage MUST detect edge as edge = 1 - (yes_price_platform_A + no_price_platform_B) and MUST NOT fire when edge <= min_edge_threshold (default 0.02, configurable via strategy-parameters.arb_minimum_edge).
all_rules.rationale: STR-02 detection_rule + default_min_edge_threshold.

all_rules.rule: Cross-platform event identity (v1) MUST be established via a manual mapping table (CSV or DB table) keyed across kalshi_ticker, polymarket_token_id, manifold_id, predictit_contract_id. Fuzzy title+close-date matching is FORBIDDEN in v1.
all_rules.rationale: OQ-05 recommended default (v1 manual mapping); fuzzy matching risks fake arbs with real paper P&L loss.

all_rules.rule: Kelly-sizing (STR-01) MUST cap computed bet_size at strategy-assigned-capital even when f* numerically exceeds 1.0 and MUST refuse to place orders when f* <= 0.
all_rules.rationale: STR-01 pitfalls: f* can exceed 1 near certainty; edge_requirement f* > 0.

all_rules.rule: Kelly-sizing default kelly_fraction parameter MUST default to 0.25 (quarter-Kelly); full-Kelly is exposed via strategy-parameters but not default.
all_rules.rationale: STR-01 pitfall: full-Kelly induces ~33% probability of halving bankroll; quarter-Kelly is the documented default.

all_rules.rule: Market-maker (STR-05) MUST pull quotes within N minutes before market-close-date (adverse-selection protection against stale-quote snipers) where N defaults to 5 and is configurable.
all_rules.rationale: STR-05 adverse_selection_protection explicit requirement.

all_rules.rule: Market-maker simulated bid MUST NOT be quoted through the real top-of-book: our_bid <= real_best_bid + slippage_budget, our_ask >= real_best_ask - slippage_budget.
all_rules.rationale: STR-05 adverse_selection_protection: never quote through the real top-of-book.

all_rules.rule: Market-maker passive quotes MUST fill in paper mode only when the real platform top-of-book CROSSES the quoted price (real best-ask <= our bid, OR real best-bid >= our ask); fill size = min(quote_size, opposing-side top-of-book size).
all_rules.rationale: OQ-08 recommended default + STR-05 pitfall: slippage-model only covers aggressive orders.

all_rules.rule: Market-maker MUST stop quoting one side when |paper-position| >= max_inventory parameter and MUST widen spread when rolling_volatility > adverse-selection threshold.
all_rules.rationale: STR-05 adverse_selection_protection + inventory_management.

all_rules.rule: Market-maker T-t (time-to-close) MUST default to 7 days when market-close-date is null (perpetual manifold markets); the Avellaneda-Stoikov skew formula MUST NOT divide by zero.
all_rules.rationale: STR-05 pitfall + fallback rule.

all_rules.rule: News-spike-fade (STR-04) MUST detect spikes on each market-data-event using a rolling 15-minute window: price_move / market_price_15min_ago > 0.10. Discrete-bucket detection is FORBIDDEN.
all_rules.rationale: STR-04 event_detector + pitfall: rolling window over all ticks.

all_rules.rule: News-spike-fade exits MUST include: take-profit at 50% retrace, stop-loss on another 50% extension, time-stop at fade_timeout_minutes (default 60), and immediate close on market-resolution. All four MUST be evaluated on every market-data-event.
all_rules.rationale: STR-04 exit_rules.

all_rules.rule: Closing-momentum (STR-03) MUST only evaluate entries when (market-close-date - now) is within (exit_buffer, window_hours]. If market-close-date - now <= 0, the strategy MUST skip the market.
all_rules.rationale: STR-03 entry_rule + pitfall: markets can close faster than window if sync missed.

all_rules.rule: Brier-score MUST use paper-fill.entry_price as the forecast probability for each closed paper-position where strategy.strategy_id != 'market-maker'. Markets resolving 'cancelled' MUST be excluded from brier N.
all_rules.rationale: MET-01 recommended_default + OQ-02/OQ-03 + INV-010 (market-maker excluded).

all_rules.rule: Sharpe-ratio tile MUST render '—' (null) while fewer than 2 daily returns are observed; annualisation factor defaults to 252 with config override.
all_rules.rationale: MET-02 pitfalls: stdev undefined for N=1; 252-day convention.

all_rules.rule: Sortino-ratio tile MUST render '—' (null) until at least one downside return is observed; target_return defaults to 0.
all_rules.rationale: MET-03 pitfalls: denominator zero when no negative returns.

all_rules.rule: Max-drawdown MUST initialise running_peak = equity[0] = simulated-bankroll, express drawdown as positive percent, and be computed with np.maximum.accumulate over the equity-curve-series.
all_rules.rationale: MET-04 algorithm + pitfall.

all_rules.rule: Win-rate MUST be computed only over paper-positions where market-resolution ∈ {yes-resolved, no-resolved}; cancelled markets excluded from both numerator and denominator. A position with realised-pnl == 0 counts as a LOSS.
all_rules.rationale: MET-05 scope + tie_handling + OQ-03 cancelled handling.

all_rules.rule: order-side = 'sell' on an existing paper-position MUST reduce that position. Opening a short position MUST be implemented as BUY on the opposite market-side (e.g. buy NO to short YES); the plugin-interface docstring MUST document this convention.
all_rules.rationale: OQ-09 recommended default.

all_rules.rule: Adapter API keys (if supplied) MUST be loaded only from a local .env file via environment variables. Adapter code MUST NOT log keys, MUST NOT return keys in any HTTP response, and MUST NOT persist keys to the database.
all_rules.rationale: SR-002 security rule; B4 boundary invariant.

all_rules.rule: Adapter ingest MUST reject any tick with market-price outside [0.0, 1.0] and emit an AdapterError rather than writing to the database (prevents FS-002).
Every adapter MUST expose last_sync (UTC datetime) and poll_interval (seconds) as queryable properties so the data-ingestion screen and the FS-006 staleness check can read them.
all_rules.rationale: FS-006 + adapter entity capabilities in semantic-spec.

all_rules.rule: Adapter rate-limit token bucket state (HS-003) MUST be kept in-process per adapter instance and MUST NOT be persisted to the database; sync-now button requests that would exceed the bucket MUST be queued, not executed immediately.
all_rules.rationale: HS-003 hidden state + SR-003 token-bucket throttling.

all_rules.rule: Platform-specific fee modelling (kalshi taker up to 3%, polymarket gas, predictit per-trade) is OUT OF SCOPE for v1 paper mode; fee-model stays at 0 by default. Any enabling of per-platform fees MUST be opt-in via strategy-parameters and MUST NOT change existing paper-fill records.
all_rules.rationale: OQ-06 recommended default: OUT OF SCOPE unless person asks.

all_rules.rule: FastAPI's auto-generated OpenAPI schema at http://127.0.0.1:8000/openapi.json is the single authoritative contract between backend and frontend. Generate TypeScript types from it (e.g., via `openapi-typescript`) into `lib/api-types.ts` rather than hand-writing types that mirror pydantic models.
all_rules.rationale: Pydantic v2 BaseModel + FastAPI `response_model` produces a deterministic OpenAPI schema. Hand-written TS types drift; generated types guarantee the Market.yes_price bounds ([0,1]) and Platform enum stay in lockstep with backend.

all_rules.rule: Regenerate the TypeScript API types (`openapi-typescript http://127.0.0.1:8000/openapi.json -o lib/api-types.ts`) whenever any pydantic schema in `backend/app/schemas/` changes. Wire this into an npm script `types:gen` and call it as part of `predev` or on backend restart.
all_rules.rationale: Without regeneration on dev-server restart, frontend sees stale field names (e.g., renaming `yes_price` -> `yes`). Making the step explicit and scripted prevents silent UI breakage.

all_rules.rule: Zod schemas on the frontend must validate JSON returned from FastAPI at the network boundary (`MarketSchema.parse(await res.json())`) and must be kept structurally identical to the pydantic models. Any field divergence is a bug to fix on the pydantic side first, not in the zod schema.
all_rules.rationale: FastAPI is the source of truth; zod is a runtime guard against schema drift and upstream adapter corruption. Fixing divergence in zod instead of pydantic silently re-introduces the drift on the next backend restart.

all_rules.rule: Every Next.js `fetch()` call into the FastAPI backend that reads price/volume data must use `cache: 'no-store'` OR `next: { revalidate: 30 }` — never the default Next 15 behavior (uncached, but silently cached in some contexts). Pick `no-store` for dynamic market detail pages, `revalidate: 30` for market list pages (matches `live_poll_interval_seconds`).
all_rules.rationale: Next.js 15 made fetch uncached by default, but route handlers and server components still apply request memoization. Prices change every 30s; caching beyond the live_poll_interval serves stale prices and breaks bankroll math.

all_rules.rule: Client Components that display live prices must use SWR (or equivalent) with `refreshInterval: 30_000`, `refreshWhenHidden: false`, `refreshWhenOffline: false`. Server Components that render the initial HTML for the same page must fetch with `next: { revalidate: 30 }`. The two paths must agree on the 30-second cadence.
all_rules.rationale: Mismatched cadences cause UI flicker on hydration (SSR shows price X, client immediately refetches and shows Y). Recharts.md §4 explicitly recommends `refetchInterval: 30_000` to match `data_refresh_policy.live_poll_interval_seconds`.

all_rules.rule: All Recharts components must live under a `"use client"` module. Next.js page.tsx files (Server Components) may import them, but never render them in a Server Component path — instead, wrap in a thin Client Component that receives pre-fetched data as props.
all_rules.rationale: recharts.md §3 flags that Recharts uses browser SVG APIs and cannot render on the server. The pattern `<ServerPage><ClientChart data={...}/></ServerPage>` preserves SSR streaming while isolating the client boundary.

all_rules.rule: Every `ResponsiveContainer` in a Recharts chart must have a parent element with explicit height (either `h-[360px]`, `style={{ height: 360 }}`, or a flex/grid cell with defined height). When embedded in a Tailwind grid cell, set `grid-rows-[auto_1fr]` on the parent so the chart gets the flex slot.
all_rules.rationale: recharts.md §3 calls out the most common Recharts bug: chart doesn't render because parent has 0 height. Tailwind's default block-level h-auto leaves this ambiguous. A verifiable rule: chart container must have a computed height > 0 in DOM.

all_rules.rule: All Recharts `<Line>`, `<Area>`, and `<Bar>` components used for real-time (polled) series must set `isAnimationActive={false}`. Animation on 30-second repolls produces a visible stutter.
all_rules.rationale: recharts.md §3 pitfall list: 'isAnimationActive={false} for real-time data to avoid animation stutter on each update.' SWR's `mutate()` triggers React re-render on each poll; Recharts animates on every re-mount of data.

all_rules.rule: Tailwind v4 theme tokens declared in `app/globals.css` (inside `@theme { --color-yes, --color-no, --color-accent, ... }`) must also be mirrored in a TypeScript file `lib/chartTheme.ts` that Recharts reads for `stroke` and `fill` props. Never hard-code hex strings inside Recharts JSX.
all_rules.rationale: recharts.md §4 explicitly recommends 'Share a chartTheme.ts object with colors matching Tailwind tokens to avoid hard-coding hex strings.' Tailwind v4 CSS custom properties cannot be consumed by SVG `stroke` attributes directly without a helper.

all_rules.rule: FastAPI's `lifespan` asynccontextmanager is the single owner of the SQLAlchemy async engine. Never call `create_async_engine()` from a router, dependency, or background task — import the module-level engine from `backend/app/db.py` (or `main.py`).
all_rules.rationale: sqlalchemy.md §1 and fastapi.md Example 1 show one engine per process, scoped to lifespan. Multiple engines exhaust SQLite's single-writer lock and Postgres connection pool.

all_rules.rule: Every route handler that touches the database must declare `session: AsyncSession = Depends(get_session)` and never create an `AsyncSession` directly. The `get_session` dependency must be an `async def` yielding from `async with SessionLocal() as session:` (not wrapped in `@asynccontextmanager`).
all_rules.rationale: fastapi.md §3 gotchas: 'DI with yield: use Depends(get_session) with AsyncIterator only; don't wrap in @asynccontextmanager.' Direct session creation bypasses FastAPI's exception-safe teardown.

all_rules.rule: SQLAlchemy sessions must never be shared across asyncio tasks, BackgroundTasks, or the polling loop. A background task that writes to DB must open its own `async with SessionLocal() as session:` block.
all_rules.rationale: sqlalchemy.md §3: 'Don't share AsyncSession across tasks. One session per request/task.' FastAPI's `Depends(get_session)` closes the session after the HTTP response; a BackgroundTask running afterward would use a closed session.

all_rules.rule: All SQLAlchemy relationship traversals in async code must be eagerly loaded via `selectinload(...)` or `joinedload(...)` in the `.options()` clause of the query. Never access `market.ticks` after the session closes; the code must query `select(Market).options(selectinload(Market.ticks))` upfront.
all_rules.rationale: sqlalchemy.md §3 gotchas: 'No implicit lazy loading in async. Accessing market.ticks after session close raises MissingGreenlet.' This is the #1 silent async SQLAlchemy bug.

all_rules.rule: `async_sessionmaker(..., expire_on_commit=False)` is mandatory for this project. Never use the default `expire_on_commit=True` — it forces a re-fetch of every attribute after commit, which in async code means re-opening the session.
all_rules.rationale: sqlalchemy.md Example 1 + §3: 'expire_on_commit=False on session factory lets you access attributes after commit without refetch — recommended for web contexts.' Especially critical when returning the ORM object as `response_model`.

all_rules.rule: Every pydantic response model in `backend/app/schemas/` that is returned from a route with `response_model=...` must set `model_config = ConfigDict(from_attributes=True)`. Missing this flag means FastAPI cannot convert ORM instances and returns 500 errors at runtime.
all_rules.rationale: fastapi.md §3: 'from_attributes=True replaces v1's orm_mode = True.' Paired with `expire_on_commit=False`, this is the two-flag pattern that lets route handlers return ORM objects directly.

all_rules.rule: All Alembic migrations for this project must use `render_as_batch=True` in `env.py`'s `context.configure(...)` call, and any column alter / drop must wrap in `op.batch_alter_table(...)`. This is non-negotiable because the project ships with SQLite as the default database.
all_rules.rationale: alembic.md §2 and §4: 'SQLite cannot ALTER COLUMN — always wrap in op.batch_alter_table(...) / enable render_as_batch=True in env.py.' A migration that works on Postgres but not SQLite breaks the local dev loop.

all_rules.rule: Alembic `env.py` must use the async pattern: `async_engine_from_config(...)` + `await connection.run_sync(do_run_migrations)`. The sync pattern (`engine_from_config` + `connection.configure`) will raise `InvalidRequestError` with aiosqlite.
all_rules.rationale: alembic.md §2 and §4: 'Async env.py requires async_engine_from_config + connection.run_sync(do_run_migrations) — plain sync pattern won't work with aiosqlite.'

all_rules.rule: Alembic `target_metadata = Base.metadata` requires an import of `backend.app.models` (or whichever module actually defines the ORM classes) at the top of `env.py`. If models import lazily, autogenerate silently misses them.
all_rules.rationale: alembic.md §4: 'target_metadata = Base.metadata must import the module defining all models, or autogenerate will miss tables.' Verifiable: `alembic revision --autogenerate -m test` must list every ORM class.

all_rules.rule: Data migrations (UPDATE statements, backfills) must be separate Alembic revisions from schema migrations (CREATE/ALTER TABLE). Never mix `op.add_column(...)` and `conn.execute(sa.text('UPDATE ...'))` in the same revision unless the update is required to satisfy a NOT NULL constraint in the same migration — in which case it must use `op.batch_alter_table` to set the column non-nullable only after the update.
all_rules.rationale: alembic.md Example 3 shows the legitimate mix case. For all others, separating schema from data means you can roll back one without the other and the migration history reads as an audit log.

all_rules.rule: CI and pre-test setup scripts must run `alembic upgrade head` before executing pytest against a non-in-memory database. The in-memory test fixture (`sqlite+aiosqlite:///:memory:`) uses `Base.metadata.create_all` directly and does NOT go through Alembic — this is a deliberate speed choice but means migration correctness must be tested separately.
all_rules.rationale: alembic.md §4: 'CI: run alembic upgrade head before tests to ensure schema is current.' pytest.md Example 2 shows the in-memory fixture bypasses migrations. Both paths must exist.

all_rules.rule: Backend integration tests must use `httpx.AsyncClient(transport=ASGITransport(app=app))` against the full FastAPI app — not Starlette's `TestClient`. Use `@pytest.mark.asyncio` or project-wide `asyncio_mode = "auto"`.
all_rules.rationale: fastapi.md §4 and pytest.md Example 3: 'Use httpx.AsyncClient with ASGITransport (starlette's TestClient is sync and has edge cases with lifespan).' ASGITransport exercises the same code path (including lifespan) as uvicorn production.

all_rules.rule: The Playwright config's `webServer` array must launch BOTH the Next.js dev server (port 3000) and the FastAPI uvicorn server (port 8000). Both must be declared with their own health URL (`http://localhost:3000` and `http://localhost:8000/docs`) and `reuseExistingServer: !process.env.CI`.
all_rules.rationale: vitest-playwright.md §3: 'Start both servers (Next.js + FastAPI) via webServer array — otherwise E2E tests hit a dead backend.' This is the dual-webServer pattern specific to full-stack Next+Python projects.

all_rules.rule: Playwright tests that verify backend error handling must use `page.route('**/api/**', route => route.fulfill({...}))` to intercept network calls, not hit a real backend with synthetic data. Tests that verify happy-path flows must hit the real FastAPI + SQLite webServer instance and seed state via `page.request.post('/api/...')` rather than `page.evaluate`.
all_rules.rationale: vitest-playwright.md §3 and Example 3: 'Avoid page.evaluate for test setup — prefer API calls or page.request to seed state.' Mocking only error paths avoids brittle tests on happy paths while keeping error paths deterministic.

all_rules.rule: Vitest cannot render Next.js Server Components. Any `page.tsx` or module using `await headers()`, `await cookies()`, or `await params` must either (a) extract its logic into a pure TS function tested separately, or (b) be tested via Playwright only. Do not try to mock React's Server Component runtime.
all_rules.rationale: vitest-playwright.md §3 Vitest gotchas: 'Server Components can't be rendered by Testing Library — they aren't real React components at runtime. Extract logic into pure functions or plain Client Components for unit testing.'

all_rules.rule: Pytest fixtures for async DB sessions must use `@pytest_asyncio.fixture` (not `@pytest.fixture`) even when `asyncio_mode = "auto"` is set in `pyproject.toml`. The auto-mode flag applies to test functions, not to fixtures.
all_rules.rationale: pytest.md §5 gotchas: 'asyncio_mode = "auto" removes the need to mark every async test; but fixtures still need @pytest_asyncio.fixture (not @pytest.fixture).' A `@pytest.fixture` on an async function silently becomes a coroutine object instead of its awaited value.

all_rules.rule: The backtest engine and the live paper-trading engine must share the same event-loop code path. The data-handler layer is polymorphic: `HistoricalDataHandler` replays tick rows from `price_ticks` table; `LiveDataHandler` pulls from the adapter polling loop. The strategy + portfolio + execution layers are identical across both modes.
all_rules.rationale: Architectural spec from semantic-spec.json and feature-discoveries.json: backtests must produce the exact same fills that live would, given identical price inputs. Divergent code paths introduce look-ahead bias and invalidate Sharpe/drawdown metrics.

all_rules.rule: Market data adapters (Kalshi, Polymarket, Manifold, PredictIt) must implement a common async interface `async def fetch_markets() -> list[NormalizedMarket]` and `async def fetch_ticks(market_id: str) -> list[NormalizedTick]`. The polling loop (a FastAPI `BackgroundTasks` task or an asyncio task started in `lifespan`) calls them on a 30-second interval and writes to `price_ticks`. Never call adapters directly from a route handler — always read from DB.
all_rules.rationale: tech-stack.json and fastapi.md Example 5: separation prevents blocking HTTP requests on upstream adapter latency. Routes read local SQLite (microseconds); the poller fills SQLite (seconds-scale network).

all_rules.rule: The polling loop for adapters must run inside the same FastAPI process as the API (started in `lifespan` via `asyncio.create_task(...)`), not as a separate worker or cron job. There is no queue (Celery/RQ/APScheduler) in the v1 stack — simplicity over durability.
all_rules.rationale: tech-stack.json deployment = local; fastapi.md §3: 'BackgroundTasks runs in the same worker after response — not durable. Use Celery/RQ/APScheduler for real queues.' For local paper-trading v1, the shared-process model is the simpler, correct choice.

all_rules.rule: Adapter responses must be normalized to the internal `NormalizedTick { ts: int_ms_utc, yes: float_0_1, no: float_0_1 }` shape at the adapter boundary. The discriminated-union pattern in typescript.md Example 6 has a Python mirror: each adapter module exports a `normalize(raw) -> NormalizedTick` function, and the polling loop calls only `normalize`.
all_rules.rationale: typescript.md Example 6: Kalshi returns `{yes_bid, yes_ask}`, Manifold returns `{prob}`. Polymarket returns `{outcomePrices: [yes, no]}`. Normalization at the boundary means the rest of the code (strategies, charts, DB) never branches on platform.

all_rules.rule: Strategy classes must implement the interface `class Strategy: def decide(self, market: Market, ticks: pd.Series, portfolio: Portfolio) -> Order | None; def on_fill(self, fill: Fill, portfolio: Portfolio) -> None`. Strategies live in `backend/app/strategies/*.py` and are auto-registered by filesystem scan at app startup via `lifespan`. Adding a new strategy file must NOT require editing a registry or restarting anything beyond `uvicorn --reload`.
all_rules.rationale: semantic-spec.json strategy plugin requirement: 'auto-register via filesystem scan; no server restart required on new plugin' under `uvicorn --reload`. pytest.md Example 1 shows the interface shape (kelly_fraction); the class form extends it with state for on_fill.

all_rules.rule: Strategy `decide()` must receive ticks as a `pd.Series` indexed by `DatetimeIndex` in UTC. Never pass raw tick dicts or NumPy arrays — the quant library (pandas-numpy-scipy.md) assumes DatetimeIndex for rolling windows, resample, and news-spike detection.
all_rules.rationale: pandas-numpy-scipy.md §3: 'Timezones: always construct DatetimeIndex with utc=True. Mixing naive and aware breaks joins.' Every metric (Sharpe, Sortino, max_drawdown, Brier) in that file assumes pd.Series input.

all_rules.rule: Quant functions (Sharpe, Sortino, max drawdown, Brier, Kelly) must never be called from inside an `async def` endpoint. Wrap in `await asyncio.to_thread(sharpe, returns)` or mount the endpoint as sync `def ...` so FastAPI runs it in a threadpool.
all_rules.rationale: fastapi.md §3 + pandas-numpy-scipy.md §4: pandas/numpy operations are CPU-bound and release the GIL only for specific NumPy ops. A blocking pandas call inside `async def` freezes all other concurrent requests (including the 30s polling loop).

all_rules.rule: SQLAlchemy `DateTime` columns must always be written as UTC, and every `datetime.utcnow` usage must be replaced with `datetime.now(tz=timezone.utc)`. The pandas layer reads these timestamps via `pd.to_datetime(df['ts'], utc=True)`.
all_rules.rationale: sqlalchemy.md Example 2 uses `datetime.utcnow` (a pitfall: returns naive); pandas-numpy-scipy.md §3 mandates `utc=True`. Mixing tz-aware (pandas) with tz-naive (SQLAlchemy default) raises `TypeError` on comparison.

all_rules.rule: The `price_ticks` table must have the composite index `(market_id, ts)` (named `ix_market_ts`). Every query that drives a chart or backtest does `WHERE market_id = ? ORDER BY ts` — without the composite index, SQLite falls back to a scan over all ticks across all markets.
all_rules.rationale: sqlalchemy.md Example 2 explicitly declares `__table_args__ = (Index("ix_market_ts", "market_id", "ts"),)`. alembic.md Example 1 creates it. This is not optional for a 30-second polling workload over months.

all_rules.rule: Bulk tick inserts must use `session.execute(insert(PriceTick).values([...]))` with a list of dicts, NOT ORM-instance `session.add_all([...])`. For SQLite + aiosqlite, the bulk-statement path is 10x faster and avoids per-row identity-map overhead.
all_rules.rationale: sqlalchemy.md Example 5 shows the bulk insert pattern; §3 notes 'SQLite + async serializes writes — do not expect real write concurrency.' Every unnecessary round-trip compounds under the single-writer lock.

all_rules.rule: Next.js route handlers that proxy the FastAPI backend (`app/api/backend/:path*`) must set `export const dynamic = 'force-dynamic'` and forward the original request's `Accept` header. Use `next.config.ts` `rewrites()` only for static, header-less proxies; prefer explicit route handlers when any header forwarding is needed.
all_rules.rationale: nextjs.md Example 3 + §3: 'Route handlers need dynamic = "force-dynamic" when you read headers or use query params that change per request.' Example 6 shows the rewrite pattern, which is header-lossy.

all_rules.rule: FastAPI `CORSMiddleware` must be added BEFORE `app.include_router(...)` calls in `main.py`, and `allow_origins` must include exactly `http://localhost:3000` in dev. Wildcard `["*"]` is forbidden because the backend will eventually carry credentials for adapter API keys.
all_rules.rationale: fastapi.md Example 1 and §3: 'CORS middleware order: must be added before routers that return errors, else responses miss the CORS headers.' An error response from a router that lacks CORS headers appears as a network error in Next.js fetch.

all_rules.rule: Tailwind v4's `@theme` block is the single source of truth for colors AND spacing; do not install `tailwind.config.js`. Any v3-style config file must be deleted before running `pnpm dev`, or the `@tailwindcss/postcss` plugin will emit conflicting stylesheets.
all_rules.rationale: tailwind.md §4: 'Don't install tailwindcss@3 and v4 plugins side-by-side — the PostCSS plugin is a separate package in v4.' and 'No more content: [] array — delete the old config file when upgrading from v3.'

all_rules.rule: Zod schemas used for frontend form validation (e.g., backtest parameters: `{ strategy_id, start, end, bankroll }`) must match the FastAPI pydantic `CreateBacktestIn` exactly: same field names, same snake_case convention, same bounds (`bankroll: z.number().gt(0)` ↔ `Field(default=10_000, gt=0)`). Use one test that round-trips a valid object through both validators.
all_rules.rationale: typescript.md Example 2 + fastapi.md Example 2: zod and pydantic v2 have equivalent validator semantics (min/max/gt/ge), but field-casing mismatches silently drop values. A contract test prevents drift.

all_rules.rule: Server Actions in Next.js (`"use server"`) that POST to FastAPI must call `revalidatePath(...)` for the page that displays the resulting state. For backtests: POST `/api/backtests` then `revalidatePath('/backtests')`. Never `router.push(...)` inside a Server Action — use the form's redirect response or `revalidatePath` + `redirect`.
all_rules.rationale: nextjs.md Example 5 shows the pattern. Without `revalidatePath`, the user POSTs a backtest but the list page shows the stale pre-submission state until next poll.

all_rules.rule: The ORDER of hydration for a live market detail page is: (1) Server Component pre-fetches `/api/markets/{id}` with `cache: 'no-store'` and the last 100 ticks from `/api/markets/{id}/ticks?limit=100`; (2) passes pre-fetched data as props to a Client Component; (3) Client Component starts SWR with `refreshInterval: 30_000` using the pre-fetched data as `fallbackData`. This avoids the loading-spinner flash on first paint.
all_rules.rationale: nextjs.md Example 4 + recharts.md §4: Server Component SSR gives instant first paint; SWR's `fallbackData` option avoids re-fetching on mount. Both the 30s cadence and the 100-tick window match the project defaults in tech-stack.json.

all_rules.rule: Every new strategy plugin file dropped into `backend/app/strategies/` must carry a sibling test file in `tests/strategies/` that (a) constructs the strategy, (b) feeds it a deterministic `pd.Series` of ticks, (c) asserts a specific decision for at least one input. The strategy auto-registration scan must skip files whose tests do not exist (fail-closed).
all_rules.rationale: semantic-spec.json requires strategies to be verifiable; pytest.md Example 1 shows the pd.Series parametrize pattern. Fail-closed registration prevents half-built strategies from firing on live paper trading.
